title: Fractional CFO
description:
**About Us**

We’re a fast\-growing financial and strategic advisory firm that supports lower middle market businesses, private equity firms, family offices, and real estate investment platforms through every stage of the business lifecycle. Our services span four verticals — Accounting \& Finance, Transaction Services, Strategic \& Exit Advisory, and Capital Advisory — covering everything from full\-service managerial accounting and outsourced controllership to buy\-side and sell\-side M\&A advisory, transaction readiness, and CFO\-level strategic support.

Whether our clients are preparing to raise capital, optimize portfolios, or navigate complex transitions, we help drive clarity, performance, and results by combining Big 4 expertise with operator practicality.

**The Role**

We’re seeking an experienced Fractional CFO to lead high\-impact financial engagements across a diverse client base. This is a client\-facing, advisory role focused on delivering executive\-level financial leadership — without the need for full\-time, in\-house resources.

Ideal candidates will have a strong command of capital structure, financial operations, and strategic planning. Experience with portfolio management, investor reporting, and transaction support — particularly in real estate or capital markets environments — is highly valued.

You’ll collaborate with founders, CEOs, and investor groups to provide insight, structure, and execution across a range of strategic finance needs. You will also oversee and mentor accounting and finance teams across your client engagements, ensuring accurate reporting, scalable systems, and aligned financial operations.

You’ll work alongside leaders across our four service verticals, often supporting clients through transactions, capital raises, and strategic transitions where multiple parts of the firm engage.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Serve as outsourced CFO for a portfolio of clients (investor\-backed, family\-owned, or real estate\-focused)
* Lead and manage accounting and finance teams (internal and client\-side) to ensure operational excellence and financial accuracy
* Lead budgeting, forecasting, and scenario modeling efforts
* Advise on capital markets strategy: debt/equity structuring, refinancing, or recapitalizations
* Analyze and optimize asset or business unit portfolios to improve performance and ROIC
* Provide monthly and quarterly financial reporting with board/investor\-ready analysis
* Support M\&A transactions, exit planning, or capital raises in coordination with our Transaction Services and Capital Advisory teams
* Develop and implement dashboards, KPIs, and reporting tools tailored to business goals
* Build trusted relationships with stakeholders through strategic insight and clear communication

**What We’re Looking For**

* 10\+ years of progressive finance experience, including Big 4 or investment banking and private industry roles
* Experience in a CFO or VP of Finance role (fractional or full\-time)
* Strong understanding of capital structure, portfolio performance metrics, and financial modeling
* Familiarity with investor\-backed, PE\-owned, or RE\-focused operating environments
* Proven ability to lead accounting and finance teams while juggling multiple client priorities
* Strong communication skills and executive presence
* MBA preferred

**Bonus Points For**

* Experience in real estate investment, development, or asset management
* Familiarity with Yardi, AppFolio, QuickBooks, NetSuite, Sage Intacct, or other finance platforms
* Background in treasury management or debt covenant monitoring
* Prior work with family offices or multi\-entity business groups
* Experience supporting buy\-side or sell\-side M\&A processes

Engagement Details

**Job Types:** Full\-time, Contract

**Location:** Remote

**Compensation:** Competitive, commensurate with experience

Benefits

* 401(k) matching
* Health insurance
* Paid time off

Application Question(s)

* What is your earliest possible start date?
* Briefly describe a client portfolio or engagement you led as a fractional CFO. What was the scope, and what outcome did you drive?

Experience

**Relevant:** 8 years (Required)

**Big 4, Investment Banking, or Consulting:** 8 years (Preferred)

**Work Location:** Remote

Pay: $175\.00 \- $225\.00 per hour

Work Location: Remote

title: Controller
description:
Seen Media is a fast\-growing, New York City\-based outdoor advertising company specializing in premium large\-format billboards and high\-impact street\-level media across major U.S. markets. We partner with leading brands to deliver bold, culturally relevant campaigns in the most influential neighborhoods nationwide.

As we continue to scale, we are seeking a highly capable and detail\-oriented **Financial Controller** to lead our accounting function and help build a best\-in\-class financial infrastructure.

**Position Overview**

The Financial Controller will be responsible for overseeing all accounting operations, financial reporting, and internal controls. This role is critical to ensuring the accuracy, integrity, and timeliness of the company’s financials, while supporting leadership with reliable data for decision\-making and external stakeholders including lenders and partners.

This is a hands\-on leadership role ideal for someone who thrives in a fast\-paced, entrepreneurial environment and is excited to help build and scale financial processes.

**Key ResponsibilitiesAccounting \& Reporting**

* Oversee the monthly, quarterly, and annual close processes
* Prepare accurate and timely financial statements
* Ensure GAAP compliance and adherence to accounting best practices
* Maintain general ledger integrity and account reconciliations

**Operations \& Controls**

* Establish and maintain strong internal controls and processes
* Manage accounts payable, accounts receivable, and payroll functions
* Improve systems, workflows, and reporting efficiencies

**Financial Oversight**

* Support budgeting and forecasting processes
* Monitor cash flow and provide regular reporting to leadership
* Partner with leadership on financial planning and performance analysis

**External Stakeholder Management**

* Coordinate with external auditors, tax advisors, and fractional CFO, banking partners
* Prepare financial materials required for lenders, refinancing, and strategic initiatives

**Team Leadership**

* Manage and mentor accounting staff (or outsourced resources)
* Help build out the finance function as the company grows

**Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field (CPA strongly preferred)
* 7–12\+ years of accounting experience, with at least 2–4 years in a Controller or senior accounting role
* Experience in media, advertising, or a multi\-entity / multi\-market business is a plus
* Strong knowledge of GAAP and financial reporting
* Experience managing audits and working with lenders or financial institutions
* Highly detail\-oriented with strong organizational and leadership skills
* Comfortable operating in a fast\-paced, growth\-oriented environment

**What We’re Looking For**

* A **hands\-on operator** who can both execute and oversee
* Someone who takes ownership of the numbers and ensures accuracy
* A professional who can **build structure while the company scales**
* Strong communicator who can interface with leadership and external partners

**Compensation**

* Competitive base salary: **$150,000 – $200,000** (commensurate with experience)
* Performance\-based bonus
* Opportunity to grow with a rapidly expanding company

**Why Join Seen Media Group**

* Be part of a fast\-growing outdoor media company in the U.S.
* Work directly with senior leadership and have real impact
* Opportunity to build and shape the finance function
* Dynamic, entrepreneurial culture with significant growth potential

Pay: $150,000\.00 \- $200,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: Hybrid remote in New York, NY 10013

title: Part-Time Staff Accountant/Fractional Controller
description:
**Seniors Home Care is seeking a detail\-oriented, experienced accounting professional to support our financial operations on a part\-time basis.**


This role is ideal for someone with a **strong accounting background who enjoys reviewing, validating, and improving financial processes** in a flexible, lower\-hour capacity. This position functions as a key internal review layer for our accounting operations, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and strong financial discipline.


This is a **hands\-on review and oversight role** focused on maintaining clean, reliable financials. You will work closely with our existing bookkeeping/accounting function, providing a second layer of review, identifying issues, and ensuring smooth financial operations. The role is structured around a **biweekly review cycle** and monthly close responsibilities.

**Hours:** \~10–20 hours per week (aligned with biweekly review cycles)

**Schedule:** Flexible

**Location:** On Site

**Reports to:** CEO

**Key Responsibilities**

* Review and audit the work of the accountant/bookkeeper on a **biweekly basis**
* Identify and resolve discrepancies, coding errors, and timing issues
* Serve as a **secondary approval** for payroll and billing cycles
* Manage updates and configuration changes within **ADP Payroll**
* Oversee scheduling software updates related to payroll inputs
* Document system changes and communicate updates to staff
* Review balance sheet and income statement on a regular basis
* Investigate and document financial variances
* **Support month\-end close and reconciliations**

**What We're Looking For**

* **Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field**
* 5\+ years of relevant accounting experience (Controller, Accounting Manager, or Senior Accountant level preferred)
* **Strong understanding of financial statements and full\-cycle accounting**
* High attention to detail and strong financial analysis skills
* Comfortable working independently with limited hours
* Strong communication and organizational skills
* Experience with ADP, Excel, and standard accounting systems

**Who This Role Is a Great Fit For**

* An experienced accountant or former controller who enjoys reviewing and refining financial processes
* Someone looking for a flexible, part\-time role with meaningful responsibility
* A professional who takes pride in accuracy, consistency, and clean financials

**Who This Role Is Not a Fit For**

* Someone seeking a purely strategic or advisory CFO\-type role
* Someone who prefers to delegate rather than review and engage in the details
* Someone looking for full\-time hours or a rapid growth track

**If you're an experienced accounting professional who enjoys bringing structure, accuracy, and clarity to financial operations, without the demands of a full\-time role, we'd love to hear from you.**

title: Accounting Manager - 100% Remote
description:
*Please note that all applications must be submitted via contact form to be considered. Applications received through Indeed will be disregarded.*

https://www.cognitoforms.com/EncurioLLP/AccountingManager

**ABOUT ENCURIO**

Encurio was built by an entrepreneur, for entrepreneurs. It shapes everything from how we think, how we work, our client experience, and who we hire.

We are a tax strategy and advisory firm built specifically for small business owners. This includes everyone from the solo operator just getting traction to the multi\-entity entrepreneur managing real complexity. We work exclusively with businesses under $20M, because that’s where **intentional strategy** makes the biggest difference.

At our firm, **tax season isn't real.** Most firms file returns and move on. We build strategy around the complexity of the business and work proactively during the year to avoid deadline stress.

Encurio is built on the belief that accounting is a team sport. We are a group of high performing, ambitious professionals who know when to lean in, but we also plan for balance between our personal and professional lives.

**ROLE DETAILS**

**Reports To:** CEO \& Operations Manager

**Team:** 1 Staff Accountant (direct report); interfaces with outside fractional CFO team as needed

**Works Alongside**: Lead Tax Advisors, Outside Fractional CFO Team

**Work Hours:** Mon–Fri, 8–4 PST

**Benefits:** Health, dental \& vision \| ST/LT disability \| Life insurance \| Unlimited PTO \| 401(k) with 4% safe harbor match \| CPA exam reimbursement

**THE OPPORTUNITY**

This is a senior role with real autonomy and real accountability. You will own a book of 50\-75 small business clients, lead a small team, and serve as the operational backbone that makes our tax strategy work executable. The work centers on month\-end close and back\-office services, with growing demand for light FP\&A as clients scale.

You will not be handed clients and managed daily. You will run your team, manage your client relationships, and make judgment calls without pulling the CEO into everything. What you will get is a firm that is building something, clients who need a steady and knowledgeable presence, and room for you to grow.

**WHO YOU ARE**

***You'll Thrive Here If...***

You have always been someone who figures things out. You read, you research, and you show up to conversations prepared because that’s just how you’re wired. You form opinions based on experience and aren’t afraid to speak up, but you're willing to evolve and grow with new information.

You understand that real business owners don’t always have the luxury of the textbook answer, and you’re comfortable helping a client make a practical, informed decision, even if it’s a workaround, as long as they understand the tradeoff and own the choice. You know the difference between cutting corners and being realistic.

You communicate before people have to ask. When something goes wrong with a client, your first instinct is to call them, not avoid them. You see proactive, quality work as the thing that prevents fires and values a team that operates in that manner.

***This Role is Probably NOT for you if...***

You need a manager to tell you what to work on next. You prefer to wait for a client to reach out before addressing a problem. You've ever avoided a hard conversation because it felt easier. You need everything perfectly buttoned up before you can move forward. You think "that's not my job" is a reasonable response. You burn out when things get busy and coast when they slow down.

**WHAT YOU'LL OWN**

**Client Service \& Technical Accounting**

* Serve as the senior technical lead on month\-end close, clean\-up engagements, and complex accounting issues across a portfolio of 50\-75 small business clients
* Deliver light FP\&A support: cash flow analysis, budget vs. actual reviews, management reporting — as clients grow and demand evolves.
* Review and QA work performed by the staff accountant; provide real\-time coaching and feedback
* Maintain proficiency in client\-facing platforms including QBO, Bill.com, ADP, Gusto, and Shopify
* Stay current on accounting standards and industry\-specific tools relevant to the client base

**Client Relationship Management**

* Own the full client lifecycle: onboarding, ongoing relationship management, scope changes, and off\-boarding
* Price out project work and service changes; proactively manage scope so the team is protected and clients feel valued
* Build authority with clients as a trusted advisor
* Identify when a client is outgrowing the CAS team’s capacity and confidently connect them to the right next resource (fractional CFO, tax strategy, etc.)
* Manage 50\-75 client relationships simultaneously without escalating routine matters to the CEO

**Team Leadership \& Firm Operations**

* Lead, mentor, and develop the staff accountant; support their technical and professional growth
* Serve as the primary liaison to the fractional CFO team, ensuring smooth handoffs and clear communication on shared clients
* Monitor team capacity and flag hiring needs at least 6 months in advance; assist in recruiting and onboarding
* Identify workflow inefficiencies and drive process improvements
* Manage overtime budgets and day\-to\-day resource allocation

**QUALIFICATIONS**

***Required***

* 10\+ years of accounting experience with at least 2 years in a client\-facing or manager capacity
* Controller\-level technical proficiency: month\-end close, financial reporting
* Demonstrated experience managing a large book of client relationships (20\+ clients simultaneously)
* At least 1,000 hours of direct client interaction, including planning meetings or situations requiring professional judgment
* Experience pricing services, managing scope, and holding commercial conversations with clients
* QuickBooks Pro Advisor Certification (or willingness to obtain upon hire)
* Proficiency in QBO, Bill.com, ADP or Gusto, and Shopify
* Comfort with Notion, Slack, and Microsoft Office Suite

***Preferred***

* Degree in Accounting or a related field
* CPA license preferred
* Experience in a CAS or outsourced accounting firm environment
* Exposure to FP\&A: light budgeting, forecasting, and management reporting
* Experience working alongside or interfacing with a fractional CFO function
* Exposure to small business owners as the primary client base. We define small as $20M and under, closely held (5 or fewer owners)

**A DAY IN THE LIFE**

No two days look exactly the same, but here’s what you can expect:

* Sync with your staff accountant to organize the day and remove team roadblocks
* Join a manager check\-in to discuss capacity, resource allocation, and any escalations
* Lead a client financial review presenting actuals, flagging variances, advising on next steps
* Coach a team member through a tricky technical issue or client situation
* Review a prospective client’s books to scope and price catchup work and ongoing services
* Spend focused time on technical close work or a clean\-up engagement
* Review books prior to month end close for quality control

*Encurio is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all team members.*

Job Type: Full\-time

Base Pay: From $90,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Disability insurance
* Flexible schedule
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid sick time
* Paid time off
* Parental leave
* Professional development assistance
* Unlimited paid time off
* Vision insurance
* Work from home

Work Location: Remote

title: Controller
description:
Controller — Bennett Financials

ABOUT BENNETT FINANCIALS

Bennett Financials is a CFO services firm for service\-based businesses doing $1M–$20M in annual revenue. We integrate bookkeeping, tax planning, tax preparation, and CFO strategy into one cohesive monthly engagement — not four separate vendors fighting over context.

Most accountants tell business owners what already happened. We tell them what's about to. The traditional model is reactive by design. We built the opposite: a proactive advisory firm that helps owners find profit they're leaving on the table, fix the structural reasons it's leaking, and build a business worth selling.

We're a team of 10\. We move fast, hold a high bar, and give direct feedback. Some people thrive here. Some don't. We'd rather both find that out early than oversell and disappoint.

THE OPPORTUNITY

We're hiring our first Controller. The role exists to close two specific operational gaps:

**One.** Decisions and context from Arron's monthly CFO client strategy meetings aren't consistently making their way to the team executing the work. We need someone sitting in those meetings — taking the discussion in real time and translating it into clear, assignable tasks the team can run with.

**Two.** Arron is currently the primary client contact across our entire book of business. That's keeping him in client delivery instead of working on the company. We need someone who can become the day\-to\-day face of Bennett Financials for clients — running check\-ins, answering questions, owning the relationship.

This is the translation layer between strategy and execution. It's also the seat where a strong operator earns the path to a CFO role at Bennett Financials over time. We're not hiring to fill a slot. We're hiring someone who can grow into more.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

**Own client engagement.** You become the day\-to\-day face of Bennett Financials for clients across the book. You move engagements forward through active communication, follow\-through, and the kind of proactive ownership that defines how we work. Clients shouldn't have to chase you. Deadlines shouldn't slip without you flagging them first. Questions shouldn't sit. You handle the regular check\-ins, manage the relationship, and free Arron to focus on growing the firm.

**Translate strategy into action.** You sit in Arron's monthly CFO client strategy meetings. You take what was discussed and develop your own task list out of it — clear directives, clear deadlines, clear assignees — and hand it off to admins to load into the team's workflow. No one should have to chase Arron after a strategy meeting to figure out what was decided. That's your job.

**Own the integrity of client financial reporting and deliverables.** What goes out the door under our name is yours to stand behind.

**Be the technical resource for the accounting team.** You answer the hard questions, provide guidance on complex work, and ensure quality across the engagements that need a sharper accounting eye.

**Provide oversight on multi\-entity client work.** Where complexity demands it, you go deep.

WHAT YOU WON'T DO

**Manage the accounting team.** Karen, our Operations Manager, owns that team's day\-to\-day management and accountability. You're a technical and quality resource for them, not their direct manager.

**Bookkeeping execution.** The team handles transactional work. You're not in QuickBooks coding transactions.

**CFO\-level client strategy.** Arron owns the strategic CFO relationship and the high\-level frameworks. You're translating and delivering on that strategy, not setting it.

We're being clear about this because muddy ownership is how good firms turn into bad ones. Your seat has clear lines. So does everyone else's.

WHO YOU ARE

You've worked in **outsourced accounting, fractional CFO firms, or multi\-client environments.** You know what it's like to context\-switch across a varied book and hold quality across all of it. A single\-company corporate controller seat isn't the right background for this role.

You're a **strong client communicator.** Polished, confident, able to hold relationships across a varied client base. You know how to deliver hard news, manage expectations, and earn trust with founders who have a lot at stake.

You're skilled at **taking ambiguous, high\-level strategic input and turning it into clear next steps.** You don't need a perfect brief to move. You ask the right questions and run with the answer.

You're **comfortable with multi\-entity client structures.** Complexity doesn't scare you.

You're **systems\-minded.** You improve process, you don't just maintain it. When you see something broken or inefficient, you fix it — and you bring the fix forward instead of complaining about the problem.

You **operate well in a fast\-moving, founder\-led firm** without needing heavy structure to be effective. You'll get direction, but you'll also have a lot of room to run. That's a feature for the right person.

You can **disagree with the founder and win — when you can show your work.** Arron is direct, opinionated, and changes his mind when someone makes a better case. The expectation is that you'll do the same.

WHO YOU AREN'T

This role isn't right for you if you:

* Need extensive structure, hand\-holding, or constant direction to be effective
* Wait for someone to tell you what to do instead of figuring it out
* Are uncomfortable making a decision and being able to explain it
* Want to stay heads\-down in the technicals and avoid the client relationship side
* Are coming from a single\-company seat and looking for something quieter
* Can't take direct feedback — Arron is blunt on purpose, and you'll need to be too

If any of those describe how you work, we're not the fit. And that's okay.

GROWTH TRAJECTORY

For the right person who demonstrates excellence in this role, there's a clear path to a CFO seat at Bennett Financials. We're a growing firm with a productized monthly CFO engagement and a real shot at becoming the premier advisory firm for service\-based companies in our space. The Controller seat is the entry point. What it grows into depends on you.

THE FINE PRINT

**Reports to:** Megan Bennett, Chief of Staff **Works alongside:** Arron Bennett (CEO), Karen Orsulak (Operations Manager), Jela Felismino (Bookkeeping Lead), Tony Richardson (Tax Strategist), and the broader accounting team **Location:** Remote (US\-based) **Hours:** 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM Eastern, Monday through Friday **Compensation:** $120,000 salary with the ability to earn bonuses and annual raises **Benefits:** PTO, holidays, medical/dental/vision insurance **Pay schedule:** Bi\-monthly (15th and last day of month, via Justworks)

**HOW TO APPLY**

Apply through Indeed and send us a message that includes:

* A short note (no more than 300 words) telling us about a time you took ambiguous, high\-level direction from a leader and turned it into something the team could actually execute on. We're looking for the *how*, not just the *what*.
* The name of one client relationship you've owned that you're proud of, and a sentence on why.

**Applications without these two pieces will not be reviewed.** We're explicit about this because following directions and putting in real effort are part of the job — and the first thing we're evaluating.

We read every application that meets these requirements. We respond to everyone we move forward with within two weeks.

Pay: $120,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Opportunities for advancement
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: Remote

title: Lead Bookkeeper and Client Liason
description:
**About Erica Day LLC**  
Erica Day LLC is the parent company of JEA Financial and GymFit Financial, two fast\-growing brands offering bookkeeping, coaching, and strategic financial guidance — specializing in eCommerce businesses and fitness companies. Our culture is fast\-paced, fun, and rooted in our core values: Quality Control, Honesty, and Community. We’re looking for a detail\-oriented **Bookkeeping Team Lead** with strong leadership experience, deep accounting knowledge, and the drive to take ownership of outcomes. In this role, you’ll act as the **client\-facing liaison** between our bookkeeping team and clients, ensuring A\+\+ client support and satisfaction while managing and mentoring your team.

**Why This Role Matters**  
We’ve built strong systems, but we need someone who can lead our team, maintain quality control, and serve as the primary bridge between clients and our bookkeepers. This key hire will help remove bottlenecks, provide excellent client experience, and allow our leadership team to focus on growth.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Oversee bookkeeping operations for multiple client accounts (primarily eCommerce and fitness businesses)
* Review and ensure accuracy in Balance Sheets, P\&Ls, reconciliations, and categorization
* Act as the main point of contact for client escallations providing proactive updates and resolving questions efficiently
* Manage and mentor a team of bookkeepers and Client Success Managers
* Being detail oriented so you'll read this and apply with your favorite color in an email, sent with your resume to the email address below.
* Provide quality control (QC) on team deliverables before they reach the client
* Maintain accountability for deadlines and performance across the team
* Help enforce standard operating procedures and lead weekly check\-ins

**Must\-Have Qualifications:**

* Expert\-level experience with QuickBooks Online
* Strong grasp of all aspects of the Balance Sheet (not just P\&L)
* Prior management or team leadership experience preferred
* High personal accountability and follow\-through without needing micromanagement
* Excellent attention to detail by emailing erica@jeabookkeeping.com (instead of or after applying on Indeed) and including your favorite color in the subject line when you apply for this role
* Exceptional communication, time management, and organizational skills

**Bonus Points For:**

* Experience with Profit First methodology
* Working knowledge of eCommerce platforms (like Shopify, Stripe, or inventory management tools)

**Perks \& Benefits**

* Profit First Certification paid in full (Value: $1,000/month)
* Flexible hybrid schedule with opportunities to work from home and flex hours once able to work independently
* Supportive team culture with structured training and systems
* Opportunities to grow into more strategic leadership as we scale

**To Apply:**  
Send your resume and a short message to erica@jeabookkeeping.com. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis. Local candidates within commuting distance to Allentown, NJ will be prioritized.

Pay: $55,000\.00 \- $65,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Paid time off
* Paid training

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Allentown, NJ 08501

title: Fractional CFO
description:
**Position Overview**

Showroom Windows \& Doors is seeking a **high\-impact Fractional CFO** to partner directly with ownership and leadership to drive financial clarity, profitability, and scalable growth.

This is not a bookkeeping or reporting role. We are looking for a **strategic operator** who can connect financials to real\-world decisions across sales, marketing, operations, and expansion.

Our objective is to build a **highly profitable, multi\-location business positioned for a $25M–$30M\+ exit**.

**Primary Objectives**

* Improve **profitability and cash flow visibility**
* Build a **scalable financial infrastructure**
* Align **sales, marketing, and operations with financial outcomes**
* Prepare the company for **future acquisition / private equity investment**

**Key Responsibilities1\. Financial Strategy \& Forecasting**

* Build and maintain **rolling 12\-month forecasts**
* Create **scenario models** for:
* Market shifts
* Pricing strategies
* Expansion (new locations)
* Translate financial data into **clear, actionable insights for leadership**

**2\. Margin \& Job Costing Optimization**

* Analyze **true gross margins** by:
* Product line (Marvin, Pella, Andersen, etc.)
* Sales rep
* Job type (replacement vs new construction)
* Identify **margin leakage** and implement controls
* Partner with leadership to define **healthy pricing floors**

**3\. Sales \& Marketing Financial Alignment**

* Evaluate **cost per lead, cost per acquisition, and ROI by channel**
* Tie marketing spend directly to **revenue and profitability**
* Help optimize budget allocation across:
* Google
* Direct mail
* TV / streaming
* Social media

**4\. Cash Flow \& Working Capital Management**

* Build and manage a **cash flow forecasting model**
* Improve:
* Collections (A/R)
* Vendor terms (A/P)
* Deposit structures
* Ensure the business can **fund growth without unnecessary strain**

**5\. KPI Dashboard \& Reporting**

* Develop a **weekly and monthly KPI dashboard** including:
* Revenue
* Gross profit %
* Net profit
* Sales close rates
* Average ticket
* Marketing ROI
* Eliminate unnecessary reporting and focus on **decision\-driving metrics**

**6\. Expansion \& M\&A Support**

* Build financial models for:
* New locations
* Acquisitions
* Assist in evaluating:
* Deal structure
* ROI and payback periods
* Support due diligence readiness

**7\. Exit \& Private Equity Readiness**

* Prepare financials for:
* Quality of Earnings (QoE)
* Investor review
* Normalize EBITDA
* Identify and improve **valuation drivers**

**Where We Need the Most Help (Focus Areas)**

We are specifically looking for support in:

* **True financial visibility** (we don’t want surface\-level reporting)
* **Understanding real margins after commissions, install, and overhead**
* **Connecting marketing spend to actual profitability**
* **Building a repeatable, scalable financial model for growth**
* **Preparing for a future exit—not just running day\-to\-day finances**

**What Success Looks Like (First 90–180 Days)**

* Clear, accurate **financial dashboard implemented**
* Reliable **cash flow forecast in place**
* Defined **margin floors and pricing strategy**
* Identified **profit leaks and corrective actions underway**
* Leadership has **confidence in financial decision\-making**

**Ideal Candidate Profile**

* Experience in **home services, construction, or high\-ticket residential sales**
* Strong background in **multi\-location growth businesses**
* Proven track record supporting **$10M–$50M\+ companies**
* Experience with **private equity, M\&A, or exit preparation**
* Able to communicate financial insights in **simple, practical terms**

Pay: $4,500\.00 \- $6,000\.00 per month

Work Location: In person

title: Controller
description:
**Financial Controller**

**Location:** Miami–Dade / South Florida — Hybrid (on\-site during ramp; 1–3 remote days thereafter)

**Reports To:** Chief Executive Officer \& Executive Suite

**Employment Type:** Full\-Time, W\-2, Exempt

**Compensation:** $110,000–$130,000 base, commensurate with experience, plus performance\-based step\-ups at 6 and 12 months and a defined path to CFO

**Relocation:** Assistance available for qualified candidates

**About Us**

We are a fast\-growing, vertically integrated hemp, CBD, and alternative wellness operator running across several entities — plus our newest THC beverage brand Amigos. Our business spans direct\-to\-consumer e\-commerce (CBD.co, DestinoFarms.com), wholesale distribution (WholesaleCBD.co, HighestGoods.com), brick\-and\-mortar retail (Canna Ocho 1 \& 2, Canna Like This), and in\-house manufacturing including white\-label and contract work. We also hold majority or full equity positions in several brands.

We employ roughly 150 team members across retail, e\-commerce / fulfillment, wholesale / sales, operations, and manufacturing — approximately 3\.5X our headcount from 2 years ago. Structurally we operate like a family office: a management company running multiple entities, multiple brands, and multiple go\-to\-market channels, each with its own P\&L dynamics.

**Why This Role, Why Now**

Our business complexity has outgrown fractional bookkeeping support. We need a dedicated, executive\-track Controller who can step into a turnaround situation, stabilize the books, build reporting and cash flow visibility from the ground up, and partner directly with the CEO and COO. This is effectively our first senior executive hire with reach beyond a single department, and we are setting the bar accordingly.

This is not a close\-the\-books\-and\-go\-home role. You will walk into a partial handoff, a backdated general ledger, and a leadership team that needs clean numbers quickly. If you are energized by that, keep reading.

**Position Summary**

The Controller will own the complete accounting, reporting, cash management, and financial planning function across all four entities and every business segment. You will consolidate entities, build segment\-level visibility, advise on corporate structure and equity arrangements, and establish the systems, processes, and AI\-driven workflows that turn our data into decisions.

This is a direct\-to\-CEO, executive\-track role with a stated growth path to CFO over the next 18–36 months as the business continues to scale.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Accounting \& Multi\-Entity Financial Operations**

* Own month\-end, quarter\-end, and year\-end close across all four entities (MIA, MLT, HG, Amigos), including the management\-company structure that operates them.
* Maintain the general ledger, chart of accounts, inter\-company transactions, and consolidation / elimination entries across entities.
* Oversee all accounts payable (Bill.com), accounts receivable, payroll reconciliation (ADP), bank and credit\-card reconciliations, and merchant processor settlements.
* Manage multi\-state sales tax compliance across e\-commerce, retail POS, and wholesale channels.
* Coordinate with our external CPA firm on annual tax filings, K\-1s, 1099s, audits, and any regulatory or banking reporting.
* Ensure proper revenue recognition across DTC e\-commerce (Shopify, WooCommerce), retail POS (Lightspeed), wholesale, manufacturing, and white\-label / contract work.
* Partner with legal on corporate structure, operating agreements, equity arrangements, distributions, and any employee equity or profit\-sharing programs.

**Financial Reporting, FP\&A, and Cash Flow**

* Produce timely, accurate monthly financial statements (P\&L, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow) at the entity, segment, and consolidated level.
* Build and own a driver\-based 13\-week cash flow forecast and a rolling 12\-month cash flow model.
* Develop segment\-level P\&Ls (Retail, E\-Commerce, Wholesale, Manufacturing, Beverage / Amigos) that roll up into a consolidated enterprise view.
* Track the metrics that drive decisions: gross margin by channel and brand, CAC and blended ad ROI, fulfillment cost per order, wholesale margin by account, manufacturing COGS, labor\-cost ratios, and four\-wall store contribution.
* Deliver weekly and monthly variance analysis (budget vs. actual, period\-over\-period) with written commentary leadership can act on.
* Build departmental scorecards aligned with an EOS / Level 10 reporting cadence.
* Support capital and strategic decisions with clear financial modeling — new store openings, SKU expansions, line closures, acquisitions, divestitures.

**Turnaround, Systems \& AI**

* Diagnose the current state of the books in the first 30 days, document the gaps, and present a prioritized remediation plan.
* Evaluate and harden the finance tech stack: QuickBooks Online, Bill.com, Lightspeed, Argyle / Shopify, Woocommerce, ADP Run \& Workforce Now, and integrations with merchant processors, 3PLs, and marketplaces.
* Identify where automation and AI should replace manual accounting work. We expect this role to materially leverage AI tools (categorization, reconciliations, close automation, cash flow forecasting, variance commentary, SOP generation) and continuously raise the bar on productivity.
* Author the SOPs for every recurring financial process so the function is auditable, teachable, and not dependent on any single person.
* Design and lead the hire of a part\-time bookkeeper to support the day\-to\-day once the Controller function is stabilized.

**Cross\-Functional Partnership**

* Serve as the financial point of contact for every department head — Retail, Wholesale, E\-Commerce / Fulfillment, Operations, Manufacturing, Marketing, HR.
* Partner with e\-commerce on platform\-level reporting, blended ad\-spend ROI, affiliate profitability, and subscription economics.
* Support wholesale leadership with account\-level margin, pricing strategy, and AR / credit decisions.
* Work with retail store managers on inventory, shrink, cash handling, and POS reconciliation.
* Collaborate with manufacturing on job costing, BOMs, raw material tracking, and production variance.
* Support HR / Operations with payroll accuracy, benefits cost tracking, 401(k) compliance, and insurance audits (general liability, EPLI, workers’ comp).

**Who We**’**re Looking For**

We want a Controller who is early in their Controller arc and on a clear trajectory towards CFO. You have grown quickly because you are exceptional, you are hungry, and you are tired of being stuck beneath layers of bureaucracy. You want direct executive access, authority to make decisions, and a business complex enough to make the next five years interesting.

You should be energized — not rattled — by walking into incomplete books, multiple concurrent priorities, new brands launching or being acquired once or twice a year, and a leadership team that moves quickly. If you need a perfect handoff and stable scope, this is not the role.

**Required experience**

* Minimum Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field from a strong academic program.
* 2–3 years at a reputable accounting firm (Big 4 or equivalent).
* 2–3 years of subsequent step\-up experience in a family office, holding company, or complex operating company, ideally at the Assistant Controller or Controller level.
* A progression signal on the resume: CPA, CFA, MBA, or a comparable credential earned along the way.
* Demonstrated experience consolidating multiple legal entities, including inter\-company eliminations and segment\-level reporting.
* Multi\-channel operating experience — some meaningful combination of e\-commerce, brick\-and\-mortar retail, wholesale distribution, and ideally manufacturing or CPG / food \& beverage.
* Proven ability to build and maintain driver\-based cash flow models (13\-week and rolling 12\-month).
* Deep working knowledge of GAAP, revenue recognition across channels, inventory accounting, and multi\-state sales\-tax compliance.
* Expert\-level Excel / Google Sheets (financial modeling, pivots, multi\-tab linked workbooks).
* Hands\-on proficiency with QuickBooks Online and Bill.com; comfort integrating with Shopify / WooCommerce, Lightspeed, and ADP.

**Hard requirement: AI fluency**

Demonstrated experience implementing AI tools in accounting or finance workflows. You should be able to describe specific tools you have deployed and outcomes you have achieved — close acceleration, automated categorization and reconciliations, cash flow forecasting, variance commentary, SOP generation, or similar. Continued self\-directed learning in this area is expected, not optional.

**Industry experience we want to see**

* Product\-based, multi\-unit operators: CPG, food \& beverage, hospitality, multi\-unit retail, cannabis / hemp / regulated consumer products.
* Family\-office, holding\-company, PE\-backed, or multi\-brand operating environments.
* We are de\-prioritizing candidates whose entire tenure has been services\-only or single\-line\-of\-business (e.g., pure real estate accounting, pure professional services).

**Soft skills and mindset**

* Hungry, entrepreneurial, “do whatever it takes” work ethic.
* Confident decision\-maker who brings recommendations, not open questions, to the CEO.
* Builder mentality: writes the SOPs, stands up the reporting, and decides where automation belongs.
* Collaborative across non\-finance operators.
* Comfortable with ambiguity, new entity launches, and scope that changes with the business.

**What Success Looks Like in the First 90 Days**

* **Days 1–30:** Complete onboarding across all four entities. Map every bank account, credit card, merchant processor, revenue channel, and vendor relationship. Produce a written diagnostic of the current state of the books, including the gaps left by the fractional handoff, and a remediation plan with owners and dates.
* **Days 31–60:** Take full ownership of the month\-end close. Deliver the first complete set of consolidated and segment\-level financials. Stand up the first weekly departmental scorecard. Publish a 13\-week cash flow forecast.
* **Days 61–90:** Establish recurring reporting rhythms with each department head. Deliver the first full budget\-vs\-actual variance report with commentary. Present a 6\-month roadmap covering systems, automation / AI, SOPs, reporting upgrades, and the case for the part\-time bookkeeper hire.

**Compensation \& Benefits**

* Base compensation: $110,000–$130,000, commensurate with experience.
* Performance\-based step\-ups at 6 and 12 months tied to defined KPIs (close quality, cash flow model stood up, consolidated financials delivered, documented cost savings, SOPs published, audit readiness).
* Room for a formal bonus structure post\-ramp tied to finance outcomes.
* Health insurance, paid time off.
* Relocation assistance available for qualified candidates (indicative $4,000–$5,000 plus 2 weeks of temporary lodging to help you land).
* Hybrid flexibility after the initial ramp (primarily on\-site through the first \~90 days while the function is stabilized).
* Direct executive access and a stated growth path: Controller → CFO as the business continues to scale.

**Equal Employment Opportunity (Florida)**

*MIA Retail Systems, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate in hiring or employment on the basis of any legally protected status, and we do not tolerate discrimination, harassment, or retaliation. We provide reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and for sincerely held religious beliefs, consistent with applicable law. If you need accommodation during the application or interview process, please let us know.*

Pay: $110,000\.00 \- $130,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) 4% Match
* Dental insurance
* Employee discount
* Flexible schedule
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Relocation assistance
* Vision insurance

Language:

* English (Required)

Work Location: In person

title: Head of Finance
description:
**Position Summary**



The Head of Finance owns Realta Fusion's financial function, building the systems, processes, and team that will carry the company from venture\-backed startup to commercial fusion energy deployment. This senior leadership role demands deep fluency across financial planning and analysis, financial operations, and strategic finance — paired with the judgment and independence to operate as Realta's primary day\-to\-day finance leader. The ideal candidate combines hands\-on execution capability with strategic credibility, partnering directly with the CEO, fractional CFO, and board while directing our external accounting firm and embedding financial rigor into every corner of the organization. The Head of Finance thrives in a fast\-paced, build\-it\-from\-scratch environment where financial decisions directly shape our path to commercial fusion. energy.



Realta Fusion is a rapidly growing start\-up developing compact magneticmirror fusion energy systems to address the biggest challenge of our time –tackling global climate change while ensuring a sufficient energy supply for atleast ten billion people on (and off) earth. We are building a team withdiverse talents dedicated to making fusion energy a *real* solutionto humanity’s most pressing problem.


**Key Responsibilities**


**Financial Planning \& Analysis**


* Own the company\-wide budgeting and forecasting process, partnering with department heads and project leads to build bottoms\-up budgets that reflect program scope, staffing plans, and capital requirements.
* Develop and maintain financial models supporting strategic decisions including fundraising rounds, site selection, capital deployment, and workforce planning.
* Produce monthly and quarterly variance analysis, surfacing actionable insights to leadership on budget performance, capital efficiency, and financial risk.
* Serve as a primary financial partner to the fractional CFO in developing board\-level reporting, investor materials, and long\-range financial planning.
* Own capital runway management, scenario modeling, and fundraising timing analysis in close coordination with the CEO and CFO.


**Financial Operations**


* Direct Realta's external accounting firm, coordinating month\-end close, reconciliations, audit readiness, and financial statement preparation.
* Own accounts payable, expense management, and spending policy compliance — ensuring financial controls scale with the company.
* Design and maintain project\-level cost tracking structures, ensuring expenses are captured at the program level to support accurate budget management across WHAM, Hammir, and The Forge.
* Lead financial compliance for government and partner agreements, including cost\-sharing requirements, allowable cost categories, and milestone\-based billing for DOE and other funded programs.
* Partner with legal and operations on vendor contract financial terms, purchase orders, and payment milestone structures.


**Strategic Leadership**


* Serve as a senior voice on financial strategy, organizational scaling, and capital allocation — contributing directly to executive decision\-making.
* Lead implementation of financial systems and controls appropriate for a venture\-backed company scaling toward commercialization (ERP, financial reporting infrastructure, approval authority frameworks).
* Support fundraising and strategic partnership due diligence, including data room preparation and investor financial Q\&A.
* Support economic development and incentive agreement processes, including financial reporting obligations tied to TIF, state, or federal incentive programs.
* Build and develop the finance team over time, hiring and mentoring as organizational needs grow.


**Qualifications**


**Education \& Experience**


* Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or a related field; advanced degree or CPA preferred.
* 8\+ years finance experience wth demonstrated ownership of both FP\&A and financial operations functions.
* Strong financial modeling skills; advance proficiency in Excel and experience with accounting or ERP platforms (QuickBooks, NetSuite, or similar).
* Experience building and managing teams.
* Experience at a deep\-tech venture\-backed startup preferred.
* Experience presenting financial information to senior leadership, boards, or investors


**Leadership \& Execution**

  

* Proven ability to recruit, manage, lead, mentor, and develop high\-performing teams.
* Comfortable in fast\-paced startup environments with incomplete information and schedule pressure.


**Compensation \& Benefits**


What we are working on is hard… and hugely important. Realta Fusion is assembling a talented team bound together by a passion to solve humanity’s biggest challenge with first\-of\-a\-kind technology. Additionally, Realta offers:


* Competitive compensation package, including equity stock options
* Comprehensive benefits including health, dental, and vision insurance, plus a 401(k)
* Flexible paid time off (PTO) to support work\-life balance
* Work and live in Madison, Wisconsin — one of America’s most livable and vibrant cities

We value diversity as a critical factor in innovation and believe a diverse team is necessary to solve the toughest problems. We aim to create an inclusive environment that unleashes the full creativity of our team members from a wide variety of backgrounds and experiences. We provide equal employment opportunities to all individuals based on merit and without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other protected status under applicable laws.

title: CFO/Controller
description:
**Job Summary**  
We are seeking a hands\-on strategic finance leader to serve as CFO Controller for our small, dynamic, and multifaceted business in State College, PA. This in\-office role combines operational accounting excellence (Controller responsibilities) with high\-level financial strategy, planning, and business partnering (CFO responsibilities). The ideal candidate will manage all financial functions, ensure accuracy and compliance, optimize cash flow, deliver actionable insights, and support growth across diverse operations, product lines, or revenue streams.

**Core Responsibilities**

* Lead full\-cycle accounting: accounts payable/receivable, payroll, general ledger, reconciliations, month\-end/year\-end close, and preparation of accurate financial statements (balance sheet, income statement, cash flow).
* Develop and manage annual budgets, rolling forecasts, and financial models; provide clear variance analysis and recommendations.
* Oversee cash flow management, banking relationships, working capital, and financing arrangements to support daily operations and strategic initiatives.
* Ensure compliance with GAAP, internal controls, federal/state/local tax requirements; coordinate tax preparation and external audits.
* Conduct financial analysis, profitability reviews, cost control, pricing strategy, and risk identification across multiple business segments.
* Act as key strategic advisor to the CEO/Owner, translating financial data into business insights for decision\-making, investments, expansions, and efficiency.
* Manage relationships with external accountants, auditors, bankers, and advisors.

**Required Qualifications**

* Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, Business, or related field; CPA, CMA, or equivalent certification strongly preferred.
* Minimum 5 years of progressive experience in finance/accounting roles, with demonstrated hands\-on expertise in financial reporting, budgeting, cash management, and compliance.
* Prior experience in small to mid\-sized businesses and/or multifaceted operations (multiple divisions, product/service lines, locations, or revenue streams) highly valued.
* Strong working knowledge of GAAP and U.S. tax principles.
* Proficiency with accounting/ERP software (Quickbooks, NetSuite, Xero, or similar) and advanced Microsoft Excel skills.
* Excellent analytical, problem\-solving, and communication abilities; capable of presenting complex financial information clearly to non\-finance stakeholders.
* High integrity, attention to detail, adaptability, and comfort thriving in a fast\-paced, entrepreneurial small\-business environment.

**Preferred Qualifications**

* 7\+ years of relevant experience, including time in a senior controller, Director of Finance, or hybrid CFO/Controller capacity.
* Track record supporting business growth, cost optimization, or operational scaling in complex small\-company settings.
* Experience with process automation, system implementations, or fractional resource coordination.

**Why Join Us?**

Be the financial cornerstone of a growing, multifaceted small business in the vibrant State College area\- home to Penn State University and a thriving community. This in office position offers broad responsibility, direct daily impact on strategy and profitability, competitive compensation starting at $125,000\+, health insurance, paid time off, and bonus potential.

Please submit your resume and a brief cover letter highlighting your experiences managing in small or multifaceted business environments and which this in\-office role interests you.

We are an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion.

Job Type: Full\-time

Benefits:

* Health insurance
* Paid time off

Work Location: In person

Pay: $110,000\.00 \- $125,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

title: Manufacturing Accounting Manager
description:
Description:

The Manufacturing Accounting Manager is responsible for plant\-level accounting execution, cost accounting integrity, and operational financial reporting in support of ElectroCraft’s Gallipolis, OH and Princeton, IN manufacturing facilities. The role focuses on accurate financial reporting, inventory integrity, and cost variance analysis, and operates under the direction of the CFO and Corporate Controller. This position reports to the CFO of ElectroCraft.


The Manufacturing Accounting Manager does not have responsibility for company\-wide consolidation, external financial reporting, or establishment of corporate accounting policy. Decision authority for manufacturing strategy, pricing, capital prioritization, and financial policy remains with Corporate Finance.

**About Our Company**


ElectroCraft, Inc. provides application\-engineered specialty fractional\-horsepower motor and motion control products. Under the hood, ElectroCraft motion systems power critical components of some of the world's most innovative products in medical equipment, robotics, industrial automation, agriculture, and lab equipment. Some of our innovative products include DC motors, gear motors, linear actuators, and motor drives. With facilities and customers around the globe, ElectroCraft provides advanced precision solutions with the highest reliability and lowest total cost of ownership, which is why it is trusted by both the largest brands and the newest cutting\-edge companies.

**ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS**:

**Functional**

* Provide financial statements with supporting schedules, perform financial analysis of the manufacturing operation, and assist in the decision\-making process and the company’s financial plans and asset management.
* Maintain standards and calculate Material, Labor, and Overhead costs in close collaboration with Operations staff.
* Responsible for maintaining cost accounting elements in Microsoft Dynamics AX ERP system.
* Prepare and analyze cost variance and profitability reports to support operational review and decision\-making.
* Provides financial analysis and reporting in support of capital expenditure reviews and post\-investment tracking.
* Support Company’s Corporate Controller and financial institutions as needed for cash management and collateral reporting.
* Maintain adequate fiscal records in support of external and internal audits.
* Maintain appropriate transfer price policies on intercompany sales and purchases.
* Review and reconciliation of month–end closing. Provide Variance vs. Budget and Forecast analysis in meaningful detail.
* Ensure inventory accuracy, develop and maintain policies for identifying, reserving, and disposal of slow\-moving and obsolete inventory.
* Monitor and post results of cycle\-count inventories; assist Operations team with analysis and reconciliation of significant variances.
* Support annual budget process at two plants: under direction of Corporate Controller and CFO
* Maintain the integrity of the financial reporting system.
* Prepare financial budgets and projections for two principal manufacturing sites.
* Practice continuous process improvement techniques by eliminating non\-value added activities.

**Leadership**

* Must demonstrate the ability to create own work direction and prioritization.
* Must demonstrate the ability to handle multiple tasks and work on several programs simultaneously.
* High levels of integrity and sense of responsibility are a must.
* Ability to adapt to a changing environment.
* Ability to effectively communicate financial information and escalate issues through appropriate management channels.
* Analytical and process\-improvement mindset within a manufacturing environment

**Interpersonal**

* Works positively with team members in order to continuously improve the business.
* Has the ability to effectively communicate with a variety of internal and external customers.
* Strong troubleshooting, analytical and reasoning skills.
* Develops and maintains positive relationships with customers, suppliers, staff, and employees.

**Business**

* Must have excellent verbal and written communication skills as well as proficiency in Microsoft Office applications.
* Possesses thorough familiarity with Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) and complies with company policies and procedures accordingly. Policies and procedures include, but not limited to: ISO 9001, government regulations (e.g. health, safety, quality and environmental), and functional policies.

**Travel Requirements**


10% \- 20% overnight travel required

*The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by people assigned to the job. They are not intended to be an all\-encompassing list of all responsibilities, duties and skills required of personnel so classified.*


Requirements:
**Education Requirements**

* 5–7 years of progressive experience in manufacturing accounting, cost accounting, or plant\-level finance roles is required.
* Strong project management and problem solving, time management and organizational skills.
* Strong cross\-functional communication skills are a must.
* Demonstrated experience with manufacturing site optimization is a strong plus.
* Experience, knowledge or exposure to Lean principles and techniques is a plus.
* CPA is a plus.

title: Accounting Team Lead (Operations)
description:
**Position:** Full\-time  
**Location:** Philippines (Remote)  
**Schedule:** 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM PST, Monday through Friday (Full\-time)

**What are we looking for:**

We are in search of a Senior Accounting Manager who will lead our growing team and work with our clients based in the United States. Our partner is a Fractional Controllership, CFO, and end\-to\-end accounting firm servicing home service and construction businesses. This is a unique company boasting a 13\-year track record of success in the Philippines and a culture built on longevity and warmth.

They are currently in a strategic growth phase, scaling our team from 15 to 30 members. We are looking for a "Big Picture" leader who prioritizes coaching and team development over task execution. You will own the entire client delivery process, ensuring our promise of excellence is met while fostering a culture of psychological safety and professional growth. Your identity is carried with pride in how you lead and elevate the skills of those around you.

Beyond your technical expertise, we are seeking a professional with impeccable English communication skills whose identity is carried with pride in how they lead, inspire, and elevate the skills of the talent around them.

**This role is 100% work from home.**

**Job Duties:**  
In this role, your responsibilities will include but not be limited to:

**Strategic Team Leadership \& Scaling**

* Growth Management: Lead and mentor a team currently at 15, with a strategic roadmap to scale to 30 by year\-end.
* Organizational Structure: Oversee the Pod Structure (5\-6 accountants per pod) to ensure workload redundancy, psychological safety, and burnout prevention.
* Mentorship: Publish and manage clear career tracks for all direct reports within the first 90 days to drive retention and professional growth with support from HR.
* Cultural Liaison: Bridge cultural and operational gaps between US and Philippines\-based teams, leading with influence and empathy.

**Operational Excellence \& Client Delivery**

* Close Management: Own the end\-to\-end client delivery process, guaranteeing zero late monthly closes.
* Utilization \& Efficiency: Manage and optimize the balance of billable vs. non\-billable hours across the team to ensure firm profitability and operational efficiency.
* Service Standards: Maintain a high standard of responsiveness, ensuring all client inquiries are addressed within a two\-hour window.
* Workload Optimization: Lead daily huddles and weekly capacity planning sessions to balance workloads across pods.
* Process Leadership: Drive accountability through influence and coaching rather than micromanagement.

**High\-Level Financial Management**

* Financial Strategy: Oversee the development of financial models, project budgets, and cash flow forecasts to support strategic decision\-making.
* Project Oversight: Manage the integrity of project cost accounting, specifically WIP reporting and job costing, ensuring accuracy across all construction projects.
* Reporting \& Compliance: Guarantee the absolute accuracy of weekly, monthly, and annual financial statements in compliance with GAAP standards.

title: Business Consulting Accounting Manager
description:
We are seeking a Manager to join the Abdo business consulting team. If you have a passion for positively impacting business clients and their accounting and finance operations, this role is for you.

  



As a Manager, you will lead a team providing accounting and financial services to our business clients within multiple industries: manufacturing, agriculture, professional services, real estate, and construction. You will oversee various consulting projects to help businesses address financial, operational, and regulatory challenges, and improve their financial efficiency.

  



Our services include fractional CFO and staffing, long\-term planning, budgeting/forecasting, process improvement, monthly close processes, special projects, and software implementation.

  



At Abdo, we’re not your typical accounting firm. For us, ‘Lighting the path forward’ means that we go well beyond traditional accounting and auditing to deliver solutions that help our clients succeed. We carefully seek out the best people and trust them to make a difference in helping our clients navigate forward with confidence.

  



Our culture is built on the following principles:


* **Relationships** \- We give our best to our clients and to each other.
* **Growth \& Development** \- We get better every day.
* **Teamwork** \- We accomplish great things together.

  


**Key responsibilities include:**


* Act as a fractional Controller for multiple clients: oversee monthly close, prepare management reports, join leadership meetings, and help improve overall business performance
* Lead special accounting projects such as budgeting and forecasting, process and internal control reviews, and KPI/dashboard development; help clients work through complex financial issues
* Advise clients on multi\-year strategic accounting and finance needs; support them through process and systems changes
* Serve as the QuickBooks Online expert: support implementations, conversions, system setup, and user training in coordination with our Financial Solutions team
* Provide guidance on the financial and tax implications of business decisions and identify when to involve the tax team for more complex issues
* Present financial results to leadership teams with clear insights
* Ensure year\-round audit readiness with clients
* Perform compilation engagements, including GAAP and tax accounting matters, financial statement preparation, and related footnote disclosures
* Lead a team providing strategic financial services to business clients
* Use top software solutions to help clients build their financial technology systems and track key performance indicators
* Manage team priorities and capacity to deliver high\-quality projects on time
* Help grow the firm by deepening relationships with existing clients, identifying additional service opportunities, and meeting with prospects to understand needs and present proposals
* Develop the team's client experience skills in conjunction with Abdo's client experience training

  


**Ideal Manager candidate has:**


* Bachelor’s degree in accounting, finance, or related field
* 5\+ years experience working in accounting; CPA preferred
* Accounting experience in the following industries preferred: manufacturing, agriculture, professional services, real estate, and construction
* Skilled in controller services, process optimization, long\-term financial planning, budgeting, cost management, and software implementations
* Experience in project and change management
* Proficient in advanced MS\-Excel functions (e.g. VLOOKUP, H\-LOOKUP, SUMIFS, conditional statements (IF, MAX, MIN), pivot tables, graphs and charts)
* Strong quantitative analysis and financial modeling skills, with a focus on improvement and automation
* Experience with Quickbooks, Syft and/or Martus Solutions preferred
* Desire to work in a fast\-paced environment
* Collaborative spirit and adaptability to change

  



Attracting, developing, and retaining the best employees are our highest priorities at Abdo. Listed as a “Fastest Growing Firm,” recognized as a "Best of the Best" firm and ranked in the Top 200 firms by Inside Public Accounting, we are always seeking talented individuals who can contribute to the continued growth and success of our firm.  

  

If you desire flexibility and control in your work schedule along with recognition for your efforts, Abdo has the opportunity you’re looking for! Apply online TODAY!

  


**Flexible Workplace:**  

Abdo approaches the hybrid work environment through flexible workplace options of working mostly from home or working mostly from the office. We want our people to work where they work best!

  



Interviews for this position may be conducted via Zoom video.

  


**Additional Information:**  

At Abdo, we are committed to providing fair, transparent, and competitive compensation that reflects the unique skills and experiences of each candidate. The estimated base pay range for this role is $95,000 \- $130,000\. Offers are typically made below the maximum to allow room for future compensation increases in the role. The compensation offer will be based on factors such as experience, education, licensure, certifications, skills, and business needs.

  



All employees have the opportunity to earn discretionary bonuses based on demonstration of extraordinary performance, special projects of significant importance, or other major accomplishments. Managers are also eligible for a manager bonus program which is awarded based on the consideration of individual contributions, team accomplishments, and firm growth.

  



Abdo cares about the wellbeing of our team members and offers a comprehensive benefits package to support this. Our benefits package includes:


* Medical, dental, vision, HSA with employer match, FSA medical and dependent care, long \& short\-term disability insurance options
* 22 days per year of PTO, 8 full holidays, 2 half holidays, 24 hours paid volunteer time, parental and grandparent leave
* 401(k) plan with employer contributions up to 4\.5%
* Abdo\-sponsored telehealth platform
* Technology reimbursement
* Half day Fridays June through September
* Professional expenses and CPA support and bonus


Benefits offerings are based off benefits eligibility requirements being met. View more information on our comprehensive benefits package on our Careers page.

  



Abdo LLP is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to any legally\-recognized basis “protected class” including but not limited to: race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, veteran status, disability, sexual orientation, marital status, or any other characteristics protected under federal, state, or local laws.

  



If you need assistance or reasonable accommodations through our recruiting process, please email us at careers@abdosolutions.com.

  



We will not be hiring candidates from agencies for this position.

title: Fractional Jr. CFO / Controller / Financial Analyst
description:
This is a video\-first role. You will be on Zoom with clients regularly and communication is a core part of the job. Presentation matters. If you are not comfortable speaking with clients on camera, this role will not be a fit.

**Compensation:**  
Base salary is $72,000 per year. Within 6–8 months, the expected total compensation is approximately $120,000 per year through client performance bonuses.

**About Us:**  
Weekly Accounting is a fast\-growing fractional CFO firm serving startups and small businesses across the United States. We grew 100% in 2025 and continue to expand. Our focus is helping business owners understand their numbers, improve profitability, and make better decisions.

We connect to all available business data including accounting systems, marketing platforms, bank accounts, and point\-of\-sale systems. We transform that data into our Integrated Financial Model (IFM), which allows clients to see their business clearly and identify what is driving growth or holding them back.

**Role Overview:**  
You will manage your own group of clients and act as their financial partner. Your primary responsibility is to interpret data, provide insight, and guide business decisions. You will not be working alone. Our internal team supports you with data preparation, bookkeeping, and model building so you can focus on analysis and communication.

**Key Responsibilities:**  
You will provide financial guidance including forecasting, budgeting, and cash flow analysis. You will work within our financial modeling system to analyze performance and identify opportunities for improvement. You will advise clients on financial processes, systems, and controls. You will lead client meetings and communicate insights clearly, helping business owners understand what actions to take.

**Requirements:**  
Candidates should have at least 1 year of experience as a CFO or Fractional CFO, or at least 3 years of experience as a Controller or in a senior financial role. Strong financial modeling experience can offset time requirements. You must have a solid understanding of accounting principles and financial reporting.

You must be proficient in Google Sheets, including building and working with formulas. Experience with QuickBooks Online is required. A basic understanding of how software systems connect, including APIs or integrations, is important.

You should be a strong communicator, comfortable speaking with clients, and able to explain financial concepts clearly. We are looking for someone who is self\-motivated, professional, adaptable, and willing to learn.

**Work Environment:**  
This is a fully remote position. All meetings are conducted via Zoom or Google Meet with video on. You will work closely with an internal team that supports your clients.

**Hiring Process:**  
After submitting your resume and cover letter, selected candidates will be asked to complete a recorded video Q\&A. Final interviews will follow.

**Final Note:**  
This role is focused on turning data into decisions. If you enjoy working directly with clients, solving business problems, and helping companies grow, we encourage you to apply.

Pay: $72,000\.00 \- $120,000\.00 per year

Work Location: Remote

title: Fractional CFO
description:
**Job description @ Questions**

**Position offered:** Fractional CFO / Client Coaching

**IMPORTANT:** PLEASE DON'T APPLY IF YOU'RE NOT WILLING TO DO THE VIDEO INTERVIEW FOR US. OUR ENTIRE BUSINESS IS ON VIDEO AND PRESENTATION MATTERS! We are aware that applying for jobs is not easy so we don't want to burn your time. All of our team members perform their jobs on video during a zoom meeting. Video is required to maintain that personal touch with the clients. Advice, have a nice background with decent lighting.

**Who are we:** We are business engineers who deconstruct business data and solve problems to increase profitability!

**How do we do it:** We connect to all business data the client has available, e.g: Facebook, (other marketing products), QBO, SAS systems, Point of Sale systems, bank accounts, etc., and manipulate that data into our (Integrated Financial Model / Forecast) proprietary software.

**What is our Objective:** Show our clients their business in a way they have never been able to see it. Figure out what's in their way of growth. Find missing / Leaking funds and advice to stop the burn. You will also be coaching the client on areas of the business that needs changes according to the IFM.

**What software must I be familiar with:** You must be proficient with Google Docs, especially Google Sheets and be able to manipulate, and create proper formulas within sheets. QBO is a must, and basic understanding of how software will connect with others.

**Who are we looking to hire:** Are you an experienced and qualified CFO or financial business coach who is looking for a flexible and rewarding career? Would you enjoy having your own book of business that you focus on? Do you enjoy working with different clients and industries and helping them achieve their financial goals? If so, you might be the perfect fit for our fractional CFO firm.

**Where will I report to work:** You will work from the comfort of your own home. Your meetings will be held on Zoom with video on.

**About us:** Weekly Accounting is growing quickly due to our dedication to fractional CFO services for startups and small businesses across the US. We offer our clients access to high\-quality CFOs who can handle various financial tasks and challenges on a part\-time or project basis. Our fractional CFOs work remotely and independently under our brand, but also collaborate with our team and our clients' teams to deliver exceptional results.

**How much can I make:** We expect our CFO's to earn over 6 figures fairly quickly.

**What is the compensation:** Our average client pays us $1,500 \- $2,000k a month for the build out of the IFM and the 2 half hour coaching sessions per month. You will get paid on average 20% of the billable. This equates to approximately $300\. \- $400 per client to hold an average of 2\-hr worth of meetings per month.

**Team:** You will also have a team around you to help support the clients. We have executive assistants and model builders working the client data to better support you in the coaching process and highly trained bookkeepers.

**As a fractional CFO, you will be responsible for:**

* Providing strategic financial guidance and analysis to your clients via our IFM (Integrated Financial Model) which includes items such as Unit Economics, financial projections, budgets, cash flow statements, and financial reports.
* Manipulating our Financial Modeling and Forecasting custom platform
* Establishing and ADVISING on implementation of financial policies, procedures, and controls that ensure the integrity and efficiency of your clients' financial operations.
* Communicating and maintaining relationships with your clients and stakeholders.

**Who gets hired for the position? Do you meet some of these qualifications?**

* A bachelor's degree in accounting, finance, or a related field would be nice but your talent is more important.
* At least 4 years of experience as a CFO or Fractional CFO or a senior financial executive in a variety of industries and sectors. If you have extensive experience in Financial Modeling, we will over look the time requirements.
* A strong knowledge of accounting standards and reporting requirements.
* A proficient use of bookkeeping or accounting software, such as QuickBooks, Xero, or FreshBooks.
* An excellent communication, presentation, and negotiation skills.
* A high level of professionalism, integrity, and ethics.
* A self\-motivated, proactive, and adaptable attitude.
* A willingness to learn new skills and technologies and stay updated on the latest financial trends and best practices.

If you are interested in joining our fractional CFO firm, please send us your resume and a cover letter explaining why you are the best candidate for the role. We will quickly review your resume and cover letter, then send you a request for a recorded video Q\&A which leads to the full interview.
