title: Chief Financial Officer
description:
**Fractional CFO \| CPA Required \| Hybrid – Burlington, VT** *Ascend Fractional Finance, LLC \| Full\-Time \| $150,000 base \+ profit sharing*

**You'll thrive here if...** You're energized by variety, comfortable switching contexts across industries, and you can parachute into a new environment, assess it quickly, and start adding value before you have all the answers. You're thorough and you build things right, but you don't need everything to be perfect before you move forward. You're the person who looks at a complicated situation and thinks, I can work with this. You want meaningful, challenging work across a range of clients and industries rather than the same set of problems at the same company year after year.

If you love helping business owners make better decisions with better financial information, this could be a great fit.

**About Ascend**

Since 2015, Ascend Fractional Finance has been the go\-to fractional finance partner for Vermont's most dynamic small businesses. Our team of seasoned CFOs, Controllers, and Accounting Managers serves over 80 local companies, giving them enterprise\-level financial leadership at a fraction of the cost of a full\-time hire.

We're a tight\-knit, high\-caliber team that takes the work seriously without taking ourselves too seriously. Our services come highly recommended by CPAs, attorneys, and business advisors throughout the region. Learn more at ascendvt.com.

**The Role**

As a Fractional CFO, you'll carry a portfolio of two to five clients at a time, typically companies with $3M to $50M in annual revenue across industries including manufacturing, biotech, CPG, ecommerce, and technology. No two days look the same, and that's exactly the point.

You'll serve as the senior financial voice for each client, helping ownership and leadership teams understand their numbers, plan for the future, and make confident decisions. You are a consultant, not an employee, which means you manage your time carefully, hit the ground running, and begin adding value immediately.

You'll also work closely with the broader Ascend team, including fellow CFOs and Controllers, giving you peer support and exposure to complex engagements you simply wouldn't encounter in a single\-company role.

**What You'll Do**

* Serve as a strategic financial partner to business owners and leadership teams
* Develop and oversee FP\&A functions including budgeting, forecasting, and cash flow management
* Prepare and present financial statements and management reports that drive real decisions
* Lead or support recapitalizations, strategic planning, and other high\-stakes financial initiatives
* Oversee accounting functions and collaborate with controllers and accounting staff at each client
* Identify and implement improvements to financial systems, processes, and internal controls
* Collaborate with external auditors, lenders, attorneys, and advisors on behalf of clients
* Contribute to internal methodology and tool development as the firm continues to grow

**What We're Looking For**

*Required:*

* Bachelor's degree in Accounting or Finance
* Active CPA license
* 10\+ years in a complex accounting or finance environment, with demonstrated upward progression
* Direct experience as a CFO, VP of Finance, or senior financial leadership role
* Strong GAAP knowledge and financial management fundamentals
* Experience with FP\&A, strategic planning, and cash flow management
* Advanced Excel skills with the ability to get up to speed quickly on various ERP platforms
* Proven ability to manage multiple priorities across multiple clients or entities simultaneously

*You'll stand out if you have:*

* Public accounting background
* Manufacturing, cost accounting, or activity\-based costing experience
* Experience supporting recapitalizations, transactions, or capital raises
* Experience in multiple industries or a multi\-client environment
* A track record of building and mentoring accounting and finance teams

**Work Environment**

This is a hybrid role. Roughly 75% of your work is remote from your home office; the remaining 25% involves on\-site visits to client locations, most within 30 to 60 minutes of Burlington. Clients range from office environments to light manufacturing and warehouse settings.

Standard business hours apply. We work hard for our clients, but we don't believe in burnout.

**Compensation \& Benefits**

* $150,000 base salary, commensurate with experience
* Annual profit\-sharing tied to individual and team performance
* 15 days PTO \+ 7 paid holidays
* Health insurance benefit (details provided during interview process)
* Flexible hybrid schedule

**How to Apply**

Submit a brief note of interest and your resume for immediate consideration. We review applications promptly and will reach out within one week to candidates moving forward. Qualified applicants can expect a two\-step conversation process.

*Ascend Fractional Finance is an equal opportunity employer and highly values diversity. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.*

description:
**About Our Team:**



Christy Paddock Advisors, LLC is continuing to grow and has a new opening for a qualified and experienced accounting professional to join our team as a CFO Consultant. You will work closely with business owners and finance teams to help streamline processes, close and reconcile financial records, manage cash, provide timely and accurate reporting, and work toward the company’s financial goals. We also help businesses buy and sell companies and complete interim, project, and assessment\-based work.



Our consultants work normal business hours, experience a variety of industries, and leverage their natural curiosities and skills to make a difference. Growth and promotions are based on ability and how fast someone wants to learn, not waiting for someone to retire. Our staff is comprised of highly skilled, quick learners, with great personalities. We enjoy our work, are excited about opportunities, and have fun!


**What You’ll Do:**

* Act as trusted financial advisor to CEOs/founders/leadership: develop strategy, monitor financial health, and drive performance improvement.
* Oversee or guide the finance function of client engagements: budgeting, forecasting, cash‑flow management, KPI dashboards.
* Implement or enhance financial controls, reporting systems and processes.
* Conduct financial and operational analysis: identify cost savings, revenue opportunities, and efficiency gains.
* Support funding rounds, acquisitions, or exit planning where applicable.
* Work across multiple client engagements simultaneously, managing scope, timelines and deliverables.
* Build relationships with clients, understand their business model, and tailor solutions accordingly.


**About You:**


* Bachelor’s degree in accounting or related field is required. CPA and/or MBA is preferred.
* 8\+ years of senior accounting experience, with at least 2 years in public accounting, is strongly preferred. Prior CFO, or similar, experience is a plus.
* Proficient in financial modeling (three‑statement models, scenario planning, breakeven/risk/sensitivity analysis).
* Proven track record of improving financial performance, streamlining operations, and advising executive teams.
* Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills—able to present complex financial information to non‑finance executives.
* Able to work independently in a remote/fractional consulting structure, managing multiple engagements and priorities. Ability to also work as part of a team.
* Possess a positive attitude, be able to produce our core focus, and exhibit our core values.

**Why Join Us:**



We’re growing rapidly and constantly evolving, allowing our team members to share their unique perspectives, solve new challenges, and own their careers. We’re committed to supporting your happiness, health, and overall well\-being by providing comprehensive benefits and perks, including:


* **Dynamic and Innovative Work:** An opportunity to play a critical role within a rapidly growing firm with a supportive and collaborative culture.
* **Growth Opportunities:** A dynamic and supportive environment where your contributions will directly impact the firm’s growth and success and share in the rewards.
* **Collaborative Culture:** A team\-oriented culture that values innovation, entrepreneurial spirit, and growth mindset.
* **Competitive Compensation:** A comprehensive salary and benefits package, including performance\-based incentives and growth opportunities.


*Christy Paddock Advisors, LLC is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.*

title: Chief Marketing Officer (CMO) - Legal
description:
**Job Title**: Chief Marketing Officer (CMO) \- Legal

**Location**: Remote

**Position Type**: Full\-time

Stealth partners with growth\-focused law firms investing significantly in marketing to drive qualified case volume and firm expansion. We are seeking a results\-driven CMO\-level marketing leader with strong legal industry experience to oversee a portfolio of up to 8 law firm clients and lead a team of Marketing Directors responsible for execution. The CMO will deliver strategy, jump in where necessary, and manage accountability and measurable ROI.

This is not a passive advisory role. This CMO will architect strategy, lead Directors, show up fully prepared to client meetings to present to law firm ownership, dig deep on performance data, and drive measurable case growth through high\-touch optimization.

**Role Overview**

The CMO is the strategic and performance leader for their assigned legal clients.

You will:

● Develop the overarching growth and paid media strategy for each firm

● Oversee strategy and performance for up to 8 law firm clients

● Lead and manage 2 Marketing Directors

● Show up fully prepared to every client meeting

● Diagnose performance issues quickly and decisively

● Drive measurable improvements in cost efficiency and case acquisition growth through data\-backed strategies

● Hold internal team members and external vendors accountable

This role requires executive presence, deep analytical skill, and hands\-on performance marketing expertise.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Marketing Strategy Development**

● Facilitate client audit to diagnose marketing challenges and overcome roadblocks

● Develop comprehensive marketing and paid media strategies aligned to firm revenue goals

● Build six month growth roadmaps and present the roadmaps to law firm ownership

● Oversee the execution of agency vendors, working with Directors to optimize paid campaigns and holding vendors accountable to objectives through performance reviews

● Remove roadblocks, offer creative solutions, and a whatever it takes attitude towards hitting client growth objectives

● Work collaboratively with other team members to share ideas, overcome roadblocks, and keep a client’s marketing program on track

● Deliver top notch presentation decks in Google Slides for clients through writing, and pulling together any required image or visual assets

● Conduct in\-depth market analysis, competitor research, and customer segmentation to inform strategic decision\-making and identify growth opportunities

● Provide strategic guidance and recommendations on positioning, messaging, ad copy, target audience identification, and go\-to\-market strategies

**High\-Touch Campaign Audits \& Optimizations**

● Work with other Stealth team members to conduct thorough audits of campaign performance across Google Ads, LSAs, Meta, YouTube and other paid channels

● Dig into keyword performance, bidding strategy, creative performance, audience targeting, and funnel conversion metrics

● Identify inefficiencies and optimization opportunities quickly

● Provide expert insight and frameworks for budget and performance forecasting, go\-to\-market strategies, vendor and executive management

● Direct budget reallocations and scaling strategies based on data to improve lead costs

**Marketing Content Development**

● Organize, develop, write, and proof high\-quality content and video scripts for various marketing channels, including websites, sales decks, email campaigns, and ad copy, ensuring brand consistency and engagement

● Craft compelling sales and advertising copy that drives conversions, enhances brand positioning, and effectively communicates key value propositions

● Plan content calendars and targeted email marketing

**Marketing Team Management**

● Manage up to two Marketing Director level team members in implementing strategic plan through tactical execution

● Guide Marketing Directors on the best ways to utilize marketing automation tools like Hubspot for workflow creation and customer nurturing, contact data importing, and campaign messaging

● Ensure high standards for analysis, reporting, and execution

● Provide expert insight and frameworks for budget and performance forecasting, go\-to\-market strategies, brand stories, vendor and executive management

● Hold Directors accountable to client performance goals

● Foster a culture of ownership and urgency

**Executive Client Leadership \& Relationship Management**

● Lead high\-level strategy and performance meetings with law firm ownership

● Manage up to 8 law firm client relationships

● Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, serving as a trusted advisor and strategic partner for their marketing initiatives

● Communicate effectively with clients to understand their goals, challenges, and priorities, and tailor marketing consulting services to meet their needs

● Evaluate client strategy plans and identify opportunities for expansion or evaluate and approve cases for resource reductions

**Qualifications**

● Minimum 10\+ years in senior marketing leadership (Director, VP, or CMO level).

● Direct experience in legal marketing (law firm or legal\-focused agency), preferably personal injury.

● Proven track record of success in driving growth for law firms investing significantly in paid media.

● Strong expertise in digital marketing channels, marketing technology, analytics and best practices. Platforms include: Google Analytics, Google Ads, Meta Ads, GA4, conversion tracking and attribution.

● Experience managing and mentoring senior marketing team members.

● Exceptional presentation and executive communication skills.

● Ability to move quickly, think critically, and operate with precision.

● Strong project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple client projects simultaneously and deliver results on time and within budget.

● Fractional or consulting experience.

● Experience working with EOS\-run businesses.

title: Chief Technology Officer
description:
**Fractional CTO / Technical Partner — Techify (Project\-Based \| Contract \+ Revenue Share)**

Techify is a boutique AI revenue infrastructure agency. We build done\-for\-you lead management systems for businesses — speed\-to\-lead SMS agents, voice AI receptionists, database reactivation campaigns, and CRM automation.

We're growing and I'm looking for the right technical partner to grow with us.

**This isn't a 9\-5 and I'm not looking for one.** This is project\-based — when there's work, we work. When there isn't, we don't. What this role offers is real freedom and flexibility. No micromanaging, no corporate structure. Just two people building something together.

**Here's how this works:** I handle everything on the business side — sales, marketing, contracts, expenses, client relationships. All of it. What I need is someone who owns the technology and fulfillment completely. When I close a deal, I want to be able to call you, walk you through what's needed, and know it's handled. Things change, timelines shift, clients throw curveballs — I need someone who can move on the fly and figure it out without being hand\-held.

**What you'll own:**

* End\-to\-end technical fulfillment on every client project — from build to ongoing maintenance
* Deploying and customizing our pre\-built snapshots and software into each client's existing CRM and tech stack
* Custom integrations with business CRMs, ensuring everything connects and runs cleanly
* Troubleshooting and adapting when things change mid\-project
* Long\-term ownership of live client systems — you build it, you maintain it
* Growing your skills and our service capabilities as we scale

title: AI Architect
description:
**About Fractional AI**
=======================


How do you turn a decades\-old dataset into an industry\-leading medical coding agent? Teach an AI receptionist to book appointments in Spanish? Automatically generate working data connectors from API docs? Build agents that write, test, and deploy their own software packages?


Fractional AI is a startup in the heart of Silicon Valley targeting the biggest greenfield opportunity in the economy: transforming companies with AI software at a scale the market is only beginning to understand. Our clients include Zapier, Airbyte, and companies from industries ranging from data infrastructure to healthcare and life sciences.


We were founded by repeat founders Chris Taylor, Eddie Siegel, and Travis May (Founder of Datavant, $7B exit), and are backed by Shaper Capital. Most of our engineers are former founders or staff engineers from top tech companies like Google and Stripe.


We're headquartered in SF with offices in NYC, Raleigh\-Durham, and Dubai.

**The Role**
============


As an AI Architect at Fractional AI, you are the technical face of our business to prospective clients.


You will combine your technical chops and communication skills to advise stakeholders ranging from CTOs to operational business leaders with limited AI exposure. You will work with our Commercial Leader, CTO, and CEO to craft winning solutions and put structure around ambiguous problems.


Ultimately, clients will get a preview of what it will be like to work with our world\-class applied AI engineering team by engaging with you!

**What You’ll Do**
==================

* **Run discovery \& design workshops** with private equity firms and enterprise prospects, surfacing high‑impact gen AI opportunities, building high\-level roadmaps, and defining requirements
* **Architect robust AI solutions,** articulate trade‑offs, and make recommendations on tools and technologies
* **Define the scope** of implementation projects including timelines, upfront and ongoing cost projections, and risks
* **Develop mockups and prototypes** so clients can visualize the future state before committing to a full build
* **Serve as a technical advisor and thought partner** to clients, including providing insights on trends and the evolving technology landscape
* **Produce persuasive artifacts** including technical briefs, current‑vs‑future process flow diagrams, and a rock‑solid hand‑off package for our delivery teams
* **Bring automation to our GTM motion** wherever you see opportunity

**What You Bring**
==================

* 5\+ years combined experience as a Software Engineer, Deployment Strategist, Sales Engineer or Solutions Architect.
* Ability to engage as a peer with senior technical leaders and make complex AI concepts accessible to non\-expert stakeholders.
* You communicate clearly and precisely, both verbally and in writing, making technical concepts accessible to different audiences.
* You do your best work in client\-facing environments.
* Familiarity with gen AI systems, the AI ecosystem, and LLMs is a plus

**Our Values**
==============

* **Overdeliver:** We are writing the playbook on how to create enterprise value with LLMs, and building our reputation as the world’s best applied AI team.
* **Overuse AI:** We go out of our way to experiment with the latest tools and share what we learn with our teammates \- building a culture at the frontier.
* **Over "engineer" the culture:** For every big decision, we ask “would this make Fractional a better or worse place for our team?”

**Location**
============

**San Francisco, CA**  

**New York, NY**


Work Arrangement: 4 days in\-person, Fridays remote

  

**Benefits**
============

* Competitive Compensation Package: Attractive salary aligned with your experience and skills.
* 401k with Matching: Invest in your future with our company\-matched retirement plan.
* Unlimited PTO: Take the time you need to relax and rejuvenate.
* Exceptional Medical, Dental, and Vision Coverage: Comprehensive health benefits to keep you and your family healthy.
* Health FSA: Manage out\-of\-pocket health expenses effectively.
* Commuter Benefits: Make your travel to work easier and more affordable.
* Life Insurance: Providing peace of mind for you and your loved ones.
* Team Events and Activities: Regular events to build camaraderie and a strong team spirit.

  

**Ready to join us?**
=====================

description:
### **About Catch Creation**



Catch Creation is a global eCommerce company built for the digital age. We create, grow, and manage online brands that inspire customers and deliver real value through exceptional products and experiences. Our team thrives in a fully remote environment, combining creativity, data\-driven strategy, and innovation to build scalable eCommerce solutions that perform.



From concept to conversion, we specialize in developing brands that stand out in competitive markets—driven by a passion for design, storytelling, and customer satisfaction. Every product we launch reflects our commitment to quality, authenticity, and growth.



At Catch Creation, we believe great ideas can come from anywhere—and our remote\-first culture allows talented people from around the world to collaborate, innovate, and bring those ideas to life.

  



**TYPE**


**Fractional / Contract**

  

**DURATION**


**6–12 Months**

  

**LOCATION**


**Remote — USA/Canada**

  

**HOURS/WEEK**


**10–20 hrs (flexible)**

  


**About Catch Creation \& Muddy Mat**
-------------------------------------



Catch Creation is a global e\-commerce company that creates, grows, and manages online brands. Our flagship brand, Muddy Mat, is a 9\-figure revenue, bootstrapped, profitable DTC pet products company the \#1 selling indoor mat for cleaning dog paws, loved by millions of pet owners across America.



We operate across TV, DTC e\-commerce, Amazon, TikTok Shop, and expanding retail partnerships with Home Depot, Costco, and Walmart. Our portfolio also includes Cloud K9 (anti\-anxiety dog beds) and Wizz (pet stain remover). We are remote\-first, lean, and move fast.

  


**The Opportunity**
-------------------



We are looking for a hands\-on, entrepreneurial Fractional CTO to provide senior technology leadership during a critical growth phase. You will work directly with the Founder/CEO and leadership team to own our technology strategy, lead our engineering team, and ensure our platforms can scale with the business.



This is not a consulting role where you write recommendations and disappear. We need someone who will embed with the team, make decisions, and drive execution.

  


**What You'll Do**
------------------


**Technology Strategy \& Architecture**


* Own and execute our overall technology roadmap and platform architecture
* Evaluate and recommend technology solutions that support multi\-channel e\-commerce growth
* Ensure scalability, security, and reliability across all tech systems
* Represent technology in leadership and strategic planning meetings

  


**Team Leadership**


* Lead, mentor, and manage our existing engineering team day\-to\-day
* Set clear priorities, KPIs, and delivery expectations for the tech team
* Identify gaps and make hiring recommendations where needed
* Foster a culture of ownership, speed, and quality

  


**Platform \& Systems**


* Oversee and optimize our Shopify multi\-store setup, including integrations, apps, and checkout flows
* Manage integrations with order tracking tools, upsell/downsell sequences, and third\-party platforms
* Audit existing tech infrastructure and implement improvements for performance and reliability
* Ensure our systems support Amazon, TikTok Shop, DRTV, and retail channel operations

  


**What We're Looking For**
--------------------------


**Required**


* Proven experience as CTO, VP Engineering, or equivalent senior tech leadership role
* Deep hands\-on experience with Shopify and e\-commerce tech stacks at scale
* Track record of building, leading, and scaling engineering teams
* Experience with multi\-channel e\-commerce: DTC, Amazon, TikTok Shop, or retail integrations
* Strong communicator who can translate between technical and business teams
* Based in the USA or Canada

  


**Preferred**


* Prior fractional or advisory CTO experience
* Familiarity with DRTV (direct response television) tech and operations
* Experience at a high\-growth DTC brand ($50M–$500M revenue range)
* Exposure to 3PL/fulfillment tech and supply chain systems

  


**Who You Are**
---------------



You've done this before you know how to step into a fast\-moving company, get up to speed quickly, and lead without needing hand\-holding. You think like an operator, not just an architect. You're equally comfortable reviewing a pull request and presenting a 12\-month technology roadmap to the CEO.



You don't hide behind process. You ship. You own outcomes. And you know that in a lean company, your impact is visible and immediate.

  


**How to Apply**
----------------



Please apply with your resume and a brief note covering:


* Your relevant e\-commerce / Shopify experience
* Examples of engineering teams you've led
* Your availability and preferred engagement structure (hours/week, start date)
* Your rate or compensation expectations

  


*We move quickly. Strong candidates will be contacted within a few business days.*

title: Sales Executive
description:
***Link to apply listed at the bottom of this Job Description. Only applications submitted through this link will be considered.***

**WHO WE ARE**

Spyglass Ops helps founders scale themselves and their visions through operations. We partner with 7\-8 figure entrepreneurs who are scaling with purpose: leaders who value impact, and a people\-first approach to growth.

Founded by Jhana Li 5 years ago, after serving as COO at two successful startups, Spyglass is a full\-service, high\-ticket operations transformation agency. Our services include Fractional COO consulting, "done for you" operations recruitment, and operator coaching. Our flagship 90\-Day Operator Program guarantees to place the right fit second\-in\-command Operator in a business in 90 days or less.

Our mission is to transform operations from a bottleneck into the highest leverage point for business impact, growth, profit \& freedom. We don't do cookie\-cutter solutions. We dive deep to understand our client’s businesses, goals, and them as a person, then we level up their team \& Ops foundation so they can scale toward their version of business and life that matters most.

**Our Core Values are:**

* **Play Like A Nerd:** We are a team of nerds, each hopelessly passionate and excited about our area of expertise. We believe that if you are an absolute nerd for what you do, then work is play. And we play hard.
* **Own Your Opportunities:** Every mistake and failure is an opportunity. See it and use it as such. Take as much ownership as you possibly can, and use it as feedback to improve. The only two words you'll never hear at this company: fault and blame.
* **Commit to Your Own Potential:** We expect this of ourselves, each other, and our clients: to relentlessly, critically be redefining and growing into the most potent version of ourselves and the impact we can have.
* **Van Talk:** Discovered while Jhana was traveling full\-time in a van through South America, Van Talk is not just a willingness and ability to speak honestly. It is a way of receiving crucial communication, not as a personal affront, but as a genuine and necessary act of service to accomplish our shared goals and highest potential.

**If our culture and mission does not resonate with you, please do not bother applying.**

**JOB DESCRIPTION**

The Spyglass Ops team is looking for a results\-driven, consultative Sales Executive to serve as our sole full\-time Closer. You will be responsible for converting qualified inbound calls into committed, right\-fit clients for our flagship 90\-day Operator Program, a high\-ticket offering with a 95%\+ client retention rate and a proven track record of transformation for purpose\-driven founders scaling from 7 to 8 figures.

This is a rare opportunity to step into a sales seat where the product, the market, and the demand are already working. Our inbound flow currently delivers 10\-15 qualified calls per week, with a 70% show rate and an 80% qualification rate. Your job is to convert that flow into revenue, at scale, with consistency and integrity.

The Closer is a highly skilled consultative seller who operates as a peer\-level advisor to 7–8 figure founders. They help right\-fit prospects make the best decision for themselves and their business, and they walk away from prospects who are not the right fit or right timing, even when revenue is on the line. This is a values\-aligned, long\-game sales role, not a hard\-close environment.

This role will lead Spyglass Ops through a meaningful transition. You will be the full\-time Closer replacing the founder, Jhana Li, in the sales seat. As such, you will be stepping into a sales process that works, but is not yet set in stone. You will be expected to actively contribute to innovating, refining, and scaling the sales systems, script, and process in close collaboration with the Head of Sales and the RevOps team.

This is a full\-time, 1099 Contractor position. This is a critical position within the business, as such, we host a rigorous, 4\-part application process.

**WHAT MAKES THIS ROLE UNIQUE**

You will be stepping into a high\-leverage sales seat backed by a proven offer, a consistent inbound flow of qualified leads, and a supportive, high\-performance team that values your input and expertise.

Unlike most closer roles, you will not be starting from zero. You will inherit a working sales machine, a $30K average deal size, and a client base that stays with us because the product delivers. You will also inherit the creative opportunity of building the sales engine from here forward, including the script, the follow\-up cadence, the objection handling library, and the optimization feedback loop with marketing and RevOps.

Your success will be measured by one ultimate outcome: consistently converting qualified inbound demand into right\-fit clients at a 30%\+ close rate, while actively improving the sales process and maintaining the peer\-level consultative standard our brand is built on.

You will also travel with our CEO approximately five times per year to live events across North and Central America, supporting her in converting qualified leads from masterminds, conferences, and speaking engagements.

**RESPONSIBILITIES (TASKS ASSOCIATED WITH THE ROLE):**

**Closing and Sales Execution**

* Take all qualified inbound sales calls booked on your calendar, currently averaging 10\-15 per week
* Run consultative, value\-based discovery and closing conversations aligned with our Ideal Client Avatar: 35–55 year old, purpose\-driven, 7–8 figure entrepreneurs
* Maximize one\-call closes on qualified shown leads, and manage multi\-call closes when prospects need additional touchpoints
* Maintain an average sales cycle of 5–10 days from first call to close
* Operate as a peer\-level advisor, helping right\-fit prospects self\-qualify and make the best decision for their business

**Pipeline and Follow\-Up Management**

* Own end\-to\-end management of your personal lead pipeline with ruthless CRM discipline
* Build and execute a rigorous, consistent follow\-up cadence for every active opportunity, ensuring no qualified lead goes cold
* Partner with the RevOps team to leverage and continuously optimize systems, automations, and AI agents that augment the sales process

**Sales Process and Script Optimization**

* Continuously test and refine the sales script, discovery framework, and objection handling
* Document insights from every call (won, lost, and no\-decision) to identify patterns and drive continuous improvement
* Surface at least one tested improvement to the sales script, qualification framework, or objection handling per month
* Engage in bi\-weekly sales training with the Head of Sales for continuous skill and performance development
* Stay sharp on our offer, ICA, competitive landscape, and the deeper business operations context that informs consultative selling

**Live Event Sales**

* Travel with the CEO approximately five times per year to live events where she is speaking to qualified rooms of 7–8 figure entrepreneurs
* Support via in\-person lead qualification, appointment setting, and closing to maximize conversion from the room
* All events are in North and Central America

**Cross\-Functional Collaboration**

* Provide regular, structured feedback to marketing on lead quality, messaging resonance, and ICA fit
* Coordinate with RevOps to flag friction points, automation opportunities, and reporting needs
* Ensure clean, complete handoff of every closed client to the onboarding and client delivery team so delivery is never blindsided by misaligned promises
* Report sales performance, pipeline health, and forward forecast to the Head of Sales on the agreed cadence

**Self\-Management and Accountability**

* Manage your personal calendar, availability, and call preparation independently
* Hold the standard of high performance and excellence without external pressure
* Bring data\-backed insights and proposed solutions, not just problems, to the Head of Sales

**REQUIREMENTS (EXPECTED PROFICIENCIES):**

**Experience Requirements (Non\-Negotiable)**

* Minimum 4 years of proven track record in remote, high\-ticket sales (deals at or above $10K)
* Demonstrated ability to consistently hit and exceed close rate targets in a consultative sales environment
* Available for full\-time engagement; this role is not designed for fractional or multi\-client closers
* Strong written and verbal communication, especially in virtual closing environments

**Highly Preferred Experience**

* Direct experience closing to our ICA: 7–8 figure bootstrapped entrepreneurs and founders
* Direct experience working inside a 7–8 figure startup business, not just selling to one
* Business acumen and operational or strategic understanding that allows for credible, peer\-level conversations with founder prospects

**Sales Excellence**

* Mastery of consultative, value\-based selling, not hard\-close or pressure tactics
* Skilled at deep discovery, diagnostic questioning, and helping prospects self\-qualify
* Calm, grounded presence on calls with high\-stakes, emotionally invested founder prospects
* Strong objection handling rooted in genuine understanding of the prospect's situation

**Critical Soft Skills (Deal\-Breakers)**

* Integrity as a non\-negotiable; you hold the standard that this role exists to help right\-fit clients make the best decision for themselves, never to close at all costs
* Willingness to walk away from prospects who are not the right fit or right timing, even when revenue is on the line
* Commitment to ethical, transparent selling that protects long\-term client LTV and brand trust
* Constant and never\-ending improvement mindset; you always believe the sales process can be sharper, the script tighter, the close rate higher
* Comfort operating with autonomy while still collaborating deeply across sales, marketing, and RevOps
* Emotional intelligence and grounded presence with founder\-level prospects

**Technical Requirements**

* Exceptional CRM hygiene and pipeline discipline, with real\-time accuracy on every lead, stage, and next action (we currently use GoHighLevel)
* Comfort working 100% remotely with strong self\-management skills
* Data\-driven approach to sales performance, with the ability to analyze call data and convert patterns into actionable improvements
* Availability to travel approximately five times per year to live events in North and Central America

**Mindset and Cultural Fit**

* You see every call (won, lost, or no\-decision) as a learning opportunity
* You thrive in environments where the process is evolving and your insights actively shape the sales engine
* You are energized by the opportunity to help inherit, refine, and scale a founder\-led sales machine into its next chapter
* You are deeply aligned with our core values and see them as how you already operate, not just aspirational statements
* You hold yourself accountable to the highest standards of performance and are resilient in the face of obstacles or failure

**BENEFITS**

* OTE: $15,000\-$25,000 USD per month, ramping up over a 90\-day period
* 1099 Contractor status, full flexibility and autonomy in how you structure your work
* 100% remote — work from anywhere
* Time Off Policy: We manage outcomes, not inputs. As such, we have an unlimited time off policy. You set \& maintain your schedule, with responsibility for ensuring your deliverables are met and pipeline commitments are honored
* Travel covered for approximately five live events per year with the CEO
* Professional Development: Bi\-weekly sales training with the Head of Sales and networking \+ growth opportunities at live events

**HOW TO APPLY**

If you are interested in this position, please submit an application at the following link.

**https://recruiterflow.com/db\_5f712a22c33a3d8ff14e412f5e6ab747/jobs/10**

Our team will review your application and get back to you shortly. We look forward to hearing from you!

description:
**Executive Coach, Investor Relations \& Pitch Readiness** Location: Remote / Hybrid \| Engagement Type: Contract / Fractional or Full\-Time

**About the Role**


We are seeking a highly experienced Executive Coach to work one\-on\-one with senior leaders, founders, and executives preparing for high\-stakes investor and financial conversations. This coach will serve as a trusted advisor and strategic partner, helping clients sharpen their narratives, strengthen their presence, and develop the confidence and fluency needed to succeed in pitch meetings, banker dialogues, roadshows, and investor relations engagements.


The ideal candidate brings deep familiarity with the investor landscape, from venture capital and private equity to public markets and investment banking, and has a track record of preparing executives to perform at their best when capital, relationships, and reputation are on the line.

**What You'll Do**

* Coach executives and founders through the full arc of investor readiness, from refining the investment thesis to polishing final delivery.
* Conduct mock pitch sessions and simulated Q\&A with rigorous, realistic feedback modeled on how investors and bankers actually probe.
* Help clients develop and pressure\-test financial narratives, including story structure, key metrics framing, and handling of sensitive topics (burn rate, churn, competitive risk, etc.).
* Advise on executive presence, communication style, pacing, body language, and how to command a room with both slides and without.
* Prepare clients for banker conversations, including capital raise discussions, M\&A processes, IPO readiness, and structured finance dialogues.
* Partner with leadership teams on messaging alignment so investors hear a consistent, credible story across the organization.
* Provide written coaching notes, session recaps, and actionable development plans after each engagement.
* Support preparation for board presentations, analyst days, earnings calls, and other high\-visibility financial communications.

**What You Bring**

* 10\+ years of experience in executive coaching, investor relations, investment banking, venture capital, private equity, or a closely related field.
* Demonstrated success coaching senior leaders through pitch processes, fundraising rounds, or capital markets transactions.
* Fluency in financial concepts, deal structures, and the language investors and bankers use — you don't just coach presence, you coach substance.
* Experience working with founders, C\-suite executives, or investor relations officers at growth\-stage or public companies.
* Exceptional communication and listening skills; the ability to give direct, candid feedback in a way that motivates rather than deflates.
* ICF certification or equivalent coaching credential strongly preferred.
* Familiarity with VC/PE fundraising processes, investment banking mandates, or public company IR programs.
* Ability to adapt quickly to different industries, business models, and personality types.

title: Legal Engineer
description:
**Who you are:**  

You are a highly motivated self\-starter looking to propel your career to the next level in a thriving and collegial professional environment.

**Who we are:**  

Tarter Krinsky \& Drogin LLP (TKD) is an entrepreneurial, growing, and highly supportive firm located in the heart of New York City. Our position as a mid\-size firm promotes a work environment that fosters relationship building inside and outside of the office.


We care about each other, our clients, the work we do, and the communities in which we live. It’s the kind of environment where every employee can develop into their best professional selves, where everyone’s contributions matter, and where each employee makes a real difference right from the start. TKD is a place to challenge, share ideas, and contribute your ideas and skills as part of an exceptional team. Each day holds an opportunity to make a real impact.


The firm is currently building a dedicated AI function — one of the most substantive investments in legal technology infrastructure a firm of our size has undertaken — and this role is the cornerstone of that effort.

**What the role entails:**  

The Legal Engineer is the bridge between TKD's attorneys and its AI infrastructure. This is not a technology role that happens to sit near lawyers — it is a legal operations role that requires deep fluency in both legal practice and AI tooling. The person in this role will own the attorney\-facing layer of the firm's AI program: designing workflows, managing pilots, running training and adoption, and ensuring that AI capabilities translate into genuine value inside legal practice.


This role works in close collaboration with the Head of AI, training co\-lead, and the incoming AI Engineer. It is a high\-visibility, high\-impact position with significant room to shape how the firm builds and evolves its AI function over time.

**Essential responsibilities include:**

**AI Workflow Design \& Implementation**

* Design and build AI\-enabled legal workflows across practice areas, including litigation, transactional, business immigration, and corporate
* Translate attorney needs into structured, repeatable workflow architectures that leverage AI platforms effectively
* Document and maintain workflow templates, playbooks, and standard operating procedures
* Identify and scope high\-ROI workflow automation opportunities across the firm
**Pilot Management**

* Serve as the primary day\-to\-day manager of vendor pilots
* Coordinate between pilot participants, vendor support teams, and firm leadership
* Collect structured feedback, develop evaluation scorecards, and synthesize results for the Fractional Head of AI and executive committee
* Manage pilot timelines, participation, and vendor communications
**AI Training \& Adoption**

* Co\-lead firm\-wide AI training and adoption program.
* Develop and deliver training sessions tailored to different practice areas and staff functions
* Host recurring office hours for attorneys seeking one\-on\-one platform support
* Identify and cultivate super users and practice group AI leads across the firm
* Support the intern desk\-to\-desk support program as a supervisor and resource
**Platform Proficiency \& Vendor Relations**

* Develop deep proficiency in each AI platform deployed by the firm — going beyond standard user training to the level of real\-time troubleshooting and workflow coaching
* Stay current on platform updates, new features, and competitive alternatives
* Maintain working relationships with vendor contacts and serve as a liaison between the firm and vendor support teams
* Support vendor evaluation processes under the direction of the Fractional Head of AI
**Quality Assurance \& Feedback Loops**

* Establish error handling protocols and quality review processes for AI\-generated outputs in legal workflows
* Maintain a structured feedback loop between attorneys, the AI function, and firm leadership
* Track and document AI use cases across practice groups — what is working, what is not, and what the data suggests about next steps
**Qualifications and skills:**

* J.D. required.
* Minimum of two years of law practice experience at a law firm, in\-house legal department, or public sector office. Active bar admission not required, but candidates must have practiced law.
* Demonstrated experience designing and deploying legal workflows on one or more AI\-native legal platforms (e.g., Harvey, Jylo, CaseBlink, Litera One, ContractMatrix, Legora, or comparable tools). End\-user familiarity is not sufficient — candidates should be able to describe specific workflows they architected, the legal process problems those workflows addressed, and how they were adopted by attorneys in practice.
* Hands\-on software development experience beyond low\-code and no\-code tooling. Candidates must be able to point to applications they have built or meaningfully contributed to in a professional or project context. Proficiency with development tools and AI frameworks is expected — including one or more of the following: LangChain, LangFlow, GitHub, Cursor, Claude Code, or OpenAI Codex. This is not a requirement for a senior software engineer; it is a requirement that the candidate has written real code, understands how software gets built, and can work credibly alongside an AI engineer on custom application development.
* Strong understanding of legal workflows across at least one practice area (transactional, litigation, immigration, or similar), with the ability to translate those workflows into structured, AI\-ready process designs.
* Excellent communication skills — able to explain technical concepts to non\-technical attorneys and translate attorney feedback into actionable technical direction.
* Highly organized, self\-directed, and capable of managing multiple workstreams simultaneously.
* Demonstrated ability to design and deliver training or change management programs.
* Familiarity with Microsoft Azure AI Foundry, Copilot, or similar enterprise AI infrastructure preferred.
* Experience with AI prompt engineering and structured workflow documentation preferred.
* Prior experience building or managing an AI, legal tech, or legal operations function preferred.
* In compliance with federal law, all persons hired will be required to verify identity and eligibility to work in the United States and to complete the required employment eligibility verification form upon hire.
*TKD is currently maintaining a hybrid schedule structured as Tuesdays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays in\-office with the option to be remote Mondays and Fridays.*

**What success looks like:**  

In the first 90 days, a successful Legal Engineer will have: onboarded and begun actively managing the firm's vendor pilots; delivered at least two attorney training sessions with documented outcomes; developed workflow documentation for at least one practice area; and established a regular cadence of feedback collection from pilot participants. By six months, they will have become the firm's go\-to resource for AI workflow questions — the person attorneys come to when they don't know how to get AI to do what they need.  

  

**What you’ll love about us:**  

We care about your professional and personal growth. Training and developing our team members are at the heart of everything we do. We are committed to cultivating development opportunities for you and helping you achieve your targeted career goals. The firm values your unique characteristics. We foster an inclusive and diverse environment where multiple perspectives are appreciated as we understand that our employees play an intricate role in the expansion and success of our firm.  

  

**Compensation \& benefits:**  

The good faith base annual salary range for this position is a minimum of $140,000 to a maximum of $200,000\. The actual salary rate offered to candidates within that range will depend on a variety of factors, including without limitation, years of relevant experience, applicable bar admissions, office location, and the candidate's overall qualifications for the position as assessed by the Firm. Market and Firm factors are also considered.


Our generous benefits package includes medical, dental, vision insurance; 401K, disability, life insurance, and Paid Time Off (PTO).

**More about the Firm:**  

TKD is a pre\-eminent full\-service firm focused on serving middle\-market and larger business clients. We provide high\-quality personalized service based on our client’s goals.  

  

The strength of the firm lies not only in its experienced attorneys and congenial atmosphere but also in our holistic approach to law – serving businesses and individuals through commercial litigation, general corporate, real estate, construction\-related matters, trust and estates, employment \& labor law, as well as more specialized areas, including securities, not\-for\-profit and education, taxation, business\-related immigration matters, international law, intellectual property, and bankruptcy, among others.  

  

Our lawyers have also garnered several prestigious recognitions for their contributions to the profession and their clients. We have been selected by Crain’s NY Business as a Best Places to Work in New York City and listed on the National Law Journal’s Hot List of the top 20 mid\-sized firms in the nation. Many of our lawyers are ranked in Chambers USA and included among Best Lawyers and Super Lawyers.  

  

TKD is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We embrace equal employment opportunity and inclusiveness. We prohibit discrimination based on, among other characteristics, race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, gender identity, and sexual orientation), parental status, national origin, immigration or citizenship status, age, disability, genetic information (including family medical history), political affiliation, status as a veteran or active military service member, and pregnancy and lactation accommodations, in our employment practices including, but not limited to, recruiting, hiring, and promoting.  

  

TKD will not accept unsolicited resumes from search firms for this employment opportunity. All resumes submitted by search firms to any employee at TKD via mail, email, fax, the Internet, or in any other form and/or method without a valid written search agreement in place will be deemed the sole property of TKD. TKD will NOT pay a fee for any placement resulting from the receipt of an unsolicited resume.

title: VFX Executive Producer (Business Development)
description:
**Overview**

Visionary FX is a boutique, U.S.\-based visual effects studio specializing in invisible effects, cleanup, and beauty work for episodic television and feature film. The studio supports productions for leading streamers and studios, including Netflix, Apple TV\+, HBO Max, Amazon, Paramount\+, and Hulu, and operates as a trusted vendor for high\-volume, precision\-driven VFX work.

As the studio enters a focused growth phase, we are seeking a senior Executive Producer to drive revenue origination across episodic and feature VFX. This is a fractional leadership role focused on new business, structured as an ongoing engagement rather than a project\-based or freelance arrangement. The role is remote, with an expectation of active engagement within the Los Angeles production and VFX community.

The Executive Producer will own the pre\-award lifecycle—from relationship development and opportunity qualification through bidding and award—leveraging existing relationships while developing new ones. Success will be measured by pipeline development, awarded work, and revenue generated against defined growth targets.

The role operates in direct partnership with the Principal, with access to senior decision\-making on pricing, positioning, and bid strategy.

**Responsibilities**

* Develop and execute a business development strategy aligned with Visionary FX’s growth objectives.
* Originate, qualify, and advance new opportunities across episodic, feature, and streaming productions.
* Build and maintain relationships with VFX Supervisors, VFX Producers, Post Producers, studio executives, production companies, and larger VFX vendors.
* Develop vendor\-to\-vendor and overflow partnerships with larger VFX and post\-production studios.
* Secure invite\-to\-bid opportunities and serve as the primary client\-facing contact through the pre\-award process.
* Partner with the Founder \& Principal on opportunity qualification, bid strategy, scope definition, and pricing.
* Lead commercial discussions around rates, scope, schedules, revision parameters, and payment terms.
* Maintain clear visibility into active pipeline, forecasted revenue, and opportunity status.
* Represent Visionary FX in relevant industry meetings, events, and networking environments.
* Provide market insight to inform studio positioning, pricing, and growth strategy.

**Qualifications**

* 7\+ years of experience in VFX business development, executive production, or senior commercial roles on the vendor side.
* Demonstrated track record of originating and closing VFX work in episodic and/or feature production.
* Established, active relationships within the U.S. production and VFX community, particularly in Los Angeles.
* Strong understanding of VFX bidding workflows, vendor selection processes, and award dynamics.
* Experience managing pipeline development and driving revenue against defined targets.
* Proven ability to operate independently in a client\-facing capacity.
* Strong commercial judgment across pricing, scope, and negotiation.

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Experience in boutique or mid\-size VFX studios.
* Background in invisible effects, cleanup, paint, or compositing\-heavy workflows.
* Experience developing sub\-vendor or overflow partnerships.
* Familiarity with industry tools such as IMDbPro, Production Weekly, or CRM systems.
* Working knowledge of studio security and compliance requirements, including TPN.

**Compensation**

Compensation for this role is competitive and structured to reward performance, consisting of a monthly retainer and commission on new business.

Estimated total annual earnings range from $120,000 to $250,000\+, depending on performance. Earnings are directly tied to revenue generated, with uncapped upside.

Specific details regarding compensation and commission structure will be discussed during the interview process.

**Why Visionary FX**

* Opportunity to build and shape the business development function within a growing studio.
* Direct partnership with the Principal on business development and growth strategy.
* High\-autonomy environment with streamlined decision\-making.
* Remote, flexible engagement structure with potential to expand scope over time.
* Performance\-driven compensation aligned with results.

**How to Apply**

Please submit:

* Resume or CV.
* Brief summary of relevant experience originating and closing VFX work, including:
* Types of productions: episodic, feature, streaming, etc.
* Nature of your involvement: origination, bidding, negotiation, award.
* Optional: recent projects or clients you helped secure; confidentiality respected.

All inquiries will be handled confidentially.

Pay: $120,000\.00 \- $250,000\.00 per year

Work Location: On the road

title: Coronary Sr Principal Territory Manager
description:
We anticipate the application window for this opening will close on \- 7 May 2026  

  

At Medtronic you can begin a life\-long career of exploration and innovation, while helping champion healthcare access and equity for all. You’ll lead with purpose, breaking down barriers to innovation in a more connected, compassionate world.


**A Day in the Life**
=====================

We are seeking a committed professional to join our team. While this is a remote position not located at a physical Medtronic site, the candidate hired will be required to reside within the territory and drive to multiple accounts throughout the region. A valid driver's license is essential for this role. This role covers a larger territory and will require regular overnight travel, with expectations of up to 50% travel as new products and therapies are launched.  

As a Senior Principal Territory Manager for Coronary, you will serve as a strategic partner and clinical expert to interventional cardiologists and cath lab teams, helping them adopt advanced coronary technologies that support confident clinical decision‑making and improve patient outcomes.


You will spend your time engaging physicians and hospital stakeholders through high‑impact sales calls, procedural case support, and advanced clinical education. In the cath lab, you will provide technical leadership during coronary interventions and physiological assessments and champion best practices for the use of Medtronic’s coronary solutions, including our newly acquired digital technology, CathWorks, the market leading Angio based Fractional Flow Reserve (FFRangio) solution.


Outside the cath lab, you will work closely with hospital administrators, purchasing teams, and cross‑functional Medtronic partners—including Enterprise Accounts, Strategic Accounts, Sales Leadership, clinical specialists, and inventory analysts—to drive access, influence pricing and contract strategies.


While this is a remote position not located at a physical Medtronic site, the candidate hired will be required to reside within the territory and drive to multiple accounts throughout the territory. A valid driver's license is essential for this role. This role covers a larger territory and will require regular overnight travel, with expectations of up to 50% travel as new products and therapies are launched.


**What You Will Be Doing**

* Lead the promotion and sale of Medtronic’s coronary portfolio, including stents, balloons, guidewires, imaging, and CathWorks technologies, across assigned territory.
* Provide technical and clinical leadership in the cath lab during coronary interventions and physiological assessments, including support for complex cases.
* Deliver advanced clinical education and training to physicians, nurses, and technicians

* Build, maintain, and leverage relationships with key opinion leaders (KOLs), senior clinicians, and executive‑level decision makers.
* Partner with Enterprise Accounts, Strategic Accounts, and Sales Leadership to shape pricing strategies, support contract negotiations, and drive system‑wide adoption.
* Develop and execute sophisticated territory and account plans that address clinical, economic, and competitive dynamics.
* Achieve and exceed quarterly and annual sales targets.
* Travel frequently to hospitals and off\-site meetings, which may require overnight stays.

**Required Knowledge and Experience:**

**Education**

* High School Diploma (or equivalent) with 12 **\+ years of relevant experience\*** , **or**
* Associate’s Degree with **10\+ years of relevant experience\*** , **or**
* Bachelor’s Degree with **8\+ years of relevant experience\***
* Relevant sales, clinical, or related experience in medical devices, medtech, healthcare, or life sciences

**Desired/Preferred Qualifications:**

Preferred candidates will demonstrate a combination of clinical expertise, commercial leadership, and strategic relationship\-building skills, including:


* Experience supporting cath labs and partnering with interventional cardiologists on complex coronary procedures.
* Ability to build credibility with physicians and healthcare leaders by delivering data\-driven, customer\-focused solutions.
* Strong territory/business planning skills, including managing competing priorities, anticipating market shifts, and executing long\-term strategies with minimal oversight.
* Proven ability to perform effectively in high\-stakes clinical environments, providing composed procedural leadership.

**U.S. Work Authorization \& Sponsorship**

At Medtronic, we are committed to fostering an environment where employees can thrive and make a meaningful impact. In alignment with our enterprise\-wide workforce planning approach, U.S. work authorization sponsorship (H\-1B, TN, J, etc.) is offered exclusively for Principal\-level roles and above, where specialized expertise aligns with long\-term business needs. Roles below the Principal level require candidates to possess unrestricted U.S. work authorization at the time of hire and for the duration of employment.


Join us in our mission to alleviate pain, restore health, and extend life—where your unique background and perspective are valued.


**Benefits \& Compensation**
============================

**Medtronic offers a competitive Salary and flexible Benefits Package**  

A commitment to our employees lives at the core of our values. We recognize their contributions. They share in the success they help to create. We offer a wide range of benefits, resources, and competitive compensation plans designed to support you at every career and life stage.

title: Director, Brand Success, TikTok Shop
description:
SuperOrdinary is a growth partner and marketplace expert helping consumer brands scale through social commerce, powered by short\-form video and livestream shopping.

**Role Overview**


The Director of Brand Success is the GM for their pod, owning full team P\&L (revenue and expenses) for a portfolio of TikTok Shop accounts and leading a team of 3–5 Brand Leads (Senior Manager level), plus shared/fractional resources as needed. This role is accountable for profitable growth, forecasting accuracy, and expense discipline while advising brands on end\-to\-end TikTok Shop strategy across lifecycle stages (launch, growth, maturity/optimization, turnaround). The Director of Brand Success is the senior escalation point for complex client situations, aligns cross\-functional teams (creator/affiliate, live, paid media, operations, finance), and sets the standard for strategic thinking and execution.


Beyond the operational rigor, we're looking for someone who genuinely lives and breathes TikTok Shop. This is a platform that changes weekly — the people who win here are the ones who are already spending their free time on TikTok, following the top creators and operators, and thinking about TTS even when they're off the clock.

**Key Responsibilities**


1\) Team P\&L Ownership (Core)

* Own end\-to\-end P\&L for your team/pod: revenue, direct costs, and operating expenses; drive profitable growth.
* Set revenue targets and manage delivery against them (retainers, commission structures, service scopes, renewals, and expansion).
* Build and manage monthly and quarterly forecasts, budgets, and variance analyses (actual vs. plan) for both revenue and expenses.
* Control and optimize team expenses, including headcount planning and utilization, contractor/freelancer spend, tools/software, and other operating costs tied to account delivery.
* Improve unit economics by brand and by workstream (affiliate, live, paid media, operations): contribution margin, gross profit, and efficiency metrics.
* Make resourcing tradeoffs to protect delivery quality while meeting profitability targets.


2\) TikTok Shop Lifecycle Strategy, GTM Architecture, and Brand Budget Ownership

* Design the end\-to\-end TikTok Shop strategy by brand lifecycle stage (launch, growth, maturity/optimization, turnaround) with clear KPIs, timelines, and owners.
* Own the brand\-level growth plan and the financial plan together: advise on brand budgets and P\&L levers (paid media spend, creator/affiliate commissions, sampling/seeding, promos/discounts, live production costs, and operational investments).
* Build business cases and scenario plans (base/upside/downside), recommend budget allocation shifts across levers, and ensure strategy decisions are grounded in ROI and profitability targets.
* Lead multi\-quarter roadmaps and GTM plans for launches, tentpoles, and major campaigns; ensure cross\-functional alignment and execution discipline.
* Partner with cross\-functional leaders to align creator/affiliate, live programming, paid media, and operations into one cohesive plan for each brand.


3\) Executive Client Leadership

* Own senior stakeholder relationships for priority accounts; coach Brand Leads on executive presence and relationship management.
* Lead QBRs/MBRs and deliver executive\-ready narratives that translate data into clear decisions and next steps.
* Proactively manage risk, expectations, and escalations; course\-correct quickly when performance, profitability, or renewals are at risk.


4\) People Leadership: Manage 3–5 Brand Leads

* Coach and develop Brand Leads to strengthen strategic thinking, prioritization, and operational rigor.
* Hold Brand Leads accountable for financial outcomes (revenue delivery, cost discipline, forecast accuracy) and client success.
* Recruit, onboard, and retain talent; run performance reviews and support career development and succession planning.


5\) Operating System \& Scalable Process

* Establish portfolio and team operating cadences (weekly business reviews, experiment logs, post\-mortems) and scalable playbooks.
* Partner with internal teams (ads, live, ops, finance) to unblock issues, ensure clean handoffs, and maintain delivery quality.
* Drive adoption of SOPs, documentation, and knowledge sharing across the team.


6\) Market \& Platform Expertise

* Bring deep market knowledge in relevant categories (beauty, fashion, personal care) and translate consumer, competitive, and cultural insights into commercial strategy.
* Stay ahead of TikTok Shop policies, algorithm shifts, category trends, and best practices; rapidly operationalize changes across your portfolio.
* Be a quick learner who can ramp into new brands, categories, and platform tools with speed and strong judgment.

**Qualifications**

* 5–8 years of experience in e\-commerce, social commerce, brand management, consulting, or high\-growth agency roles — ideally with significant time at a DTC growth agency serving beauty or personal care brands.
* Strong personal fluency with TikTok Shop. Hands\-on TTS experience is ideal; at minimum, you're actively on the platform, follow the top creators and operators, and can speak credibly about what's working in TTS right now. Bonus if you run your own shop or have been experimenting on the side.
* 2\+ years managing people or leading teams, including performance management and coaching. Experience managing managers is a plus but not required — we're open to strong senior ICs ready for the step up.
* Proven experience owning a P\&L or significant channel/account budget that includes both revenue and expense management (budgeting, forecasting, staffing/utilization, variance management).
* Deep category/market knowledge in beauty, fashion, and/or personal care, with strong commercial instincts.
* Strong analytical rigor: KPI diagnosis, funnel analysis, test\-and\-learn frameworks, and action\-oriented insights.
* Exceptional executive communication (written, verbal, decks) and comfort influencing senior stakeholders.
* High ownership, high standards, and low\-ego collaboration in a fast\-changing environment.
* Smart, scrappy, and willing to learn — you enjoy operating in a category that changes weekly.
* Plus: strategic consulting experience and/or in\-house consumer brand experience (e\-commerce, growth, or marketplace roles).

**What We Offer**

* Competitive base salary ($130,000–$165,000\) plus performance\-based annual bonus (DOE).
* Medical, dental, and vision benefits.
* 401(k) plan.
* Flexible PTO, paid holidays, and paid sick leave.
* Hybrid work schedule (3 days in office, 2 remote) and a collaborative, high\-growth environment.

title: B2B Account Executive
description:
Description:
**About Us:**


My Valuable Business, LLC (MVB) is a 25\+ year professional services firm providing fractional CFO, accounting, marketing, HR, and business advisory services to small and midsize organizations. We partner closely with our clients to help them grow, operate more effectively, and maximize long\-term value.


Our newest offering, the **O2M Gameplan**, is an executive\-level strategic planning engagement designed to help business owners and leadership teams assess business health, identify growth constraints, and build a clear 12\-month roadmap for execution.

  

We are hiring an **Account Executive** to join our Revenue Team. This is a **client\-facing, consultative sales role** responsible for leading discovery conversations, qualifying opportunities, and closing new business for the O2M Gameplan. This role works closely with our internal leadership and delivery teams to ensure a strong handoff from sales to execution.


This position is **part\-time to start**, with a clear path to **full\-time as pipeline and deal volume grow**. This position pays $36,000\-$45,000 (DOE) \+ commission.

  

This position is ideal for someone who enjoys:

* Leading strategic business conversations with owners and executives
* Diagnosing business challenges and uncovering growth opportunities
* Owning the full sales cycle from first conversation through close
* Working in a fast\-moving, entrepreneurial environment

**Key Duties:**

### **Sales \& Client Engagement**

* Serve as the primary point of contact for prospective clients throughout the sales process
* Lead discovery calls with SMB owners and leadership teams
* Identify client needs, challenges, and strategic priorities
* Clearly communicate the value of the O2M Gameplan

### **Qualification \& Pipeline Management**

* Evaluate inbound and outbound leads to determine fit
* Apply defined qualification criteria to ensure high\-quality opportunities
* Maintain accurate pipeline tracking and deal notes
* Manage follow\-ups and move opportunities through the sales process

### **Closing \& Revenue Generation**

* Own the full sales cycle from initial conversation through signed agreement
* Present recommendations and proposals to prospective clients
* Handle objections and guide prospects toward decision\-making
* Meet or exceed defined revenue and close rate targets

### **Collaboration \& Handoff**

* Partner with internal teams to ensure smooth transition from sales to delivery
* Communicate client expectations, scope, and key insights to the delivery team
* Provide feedback to leadership on messaging, positioning, and market response

### **Process Improvement**

* Contribute to refining sales scripts, messaging, and qualification criteria
* Share insights from client conversations to improve go\-to\-market strategy
* Help build repeatable, scalable sales processes


Requirements:
* 4\+ years of experience in sales, business development, or client\-facing roles
* Proven experience closing deals in a consultative or services\-based environment
* Experience selling consulting, advisory, or professional services
* Background working with small to midsize businesses (SMB market)
* Comfortable engaging with business owners, executives, and leadership teams
* Lead generation a plus, but not required
* Strong ability to lead discovery conversations and uncover business needs
* Excellent communication and presentation skills (verbal and written)
* Highly organized with strong follow\-up and pipeline management discipline
* Self\-starter with the ability to work independently in a remote environment
* Must be legally authorized to work in the United States without sponsorship

 **What We Offer:**

* Paid Time Off (PTO): Partial PTO accrual to support work\-life balance.
* Paid Federal Holidays: Time off for all federal holidays to relax and recharge.
* Thanksgiving Week Off: Enjoy time off during the week of Thanksgiving.
* Year\-End Closure: Our company is closed from Christmas Eve until after New Year’s Day, allowing you to spend quality time with loved ones.
* Career Advancement Opportunities: Access to training and development programs that encourage professional growth and career progression.
* Fully Remote and Flex Work Environment: Enjoy the freedom to work from anywhere within the U.S., with flexible start and stop times to help you balance your professional and personal life.
